chapters.zh: 乌斯地有一个人名叫约伯；那人完全正直，敬畏神，远离恶事。
chapters.en: There was a man in the land of Uz, whose name Job; and that man was perfect and upright, and one that feared God, and eschewed evil.

chapters.zh: 他生了七个儿子，三个女儿。
chapters.en: And there were born unto him seven sons and three daughters.

chapters.zh: 他的家产有七千羊，三千骆驼，五百对牛，五百母驴，并有许多仆婢。这人在东方人中就为至大。
chapters.en: His substance also was seven thousand sheep, and three thousand camels, and five hundred yoke of oxen, and five hundred she asses, and a very great household; so that this man was the greatest of all the men of the east.

chapters.zh: 他的儿子按著日子各在自己家里设摆筵宴，就打发人去，请了他们的三个姊妹来，与他们一同吃喝。
chapters.en: And his sons went and feasted houses, every one his day; and sent and called for their three sisters to eat and to drink with them.

chapters.zh: 筵宴的日子过了，约伯打发人去叫他们自洁。他清早起来，按著他们众人的数目献燔祭；因为他说：恐怕我儿子犯了罪，心中弃掉神。约伯常常这样行。
chapters.en: And it was so, when the days of feasting were gone about, that Job sent and sanctified them, and rose up early in the morning, and offered burnt offerings to the number of them all: for Job said, It may be that my sons have sinned, and cursed God in their hearts. Thus did Job continually.

chapters.zh: 有一天，神的众子来侍立在耶和华面前，撒但也来在其中。
chapters.en: Now there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD, and Satan came also among them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华问撒但说：「你从哪里来？」撒但回答说：「我从地上走来走去，往返而来。」
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Satan, Whence comest thou? Then Satan answered the LORD, and said, From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.

chapters.zh: 耶和华问撒但说：「你曾用心察看我的仆人约伯没有？地上再没有人像他完全正直，敬畏神，远离恶事。」
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that feareth God, and escheweth evil?

chapters.zh: 撒但回答耶和华说：约伯敬畏神，岂是无故呢？
chapters.en: Then Satan answered the LORD, and said, Doth Job fear God for nought?

chapters.zh: 你岂不是四面圈上篱笆围护他和他的家，并他一切所有的吗？他手所做的都蒙你赐福；他的家产也在地上增多。
chapters.en: Hast not thou made an hedge about him, and about his house, and about all that he hath on every side? thou hast blessed the work of his hands, and his substance is increased in the land.

chapters.zh: 你且伸手毁他一切所有的；他必当面弃掉你。」
chapters.en: But put forth thine hand now, and touch all that he hath, and he will curse thee to thy face.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对撒但说：「凡他所有的都在你手中；只是不可伸手加害于他。」于是撒但从耶和华面前退去。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Satan, Behold, all that he hath in thy power; only upon himself put not forth thine hand. So Satan went forth from the presence of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 有一天，约伯的儿女正在他们长兄的家里吃饭喝酒，
chapters.en: And there was a day when his sons and his daughters eating and drinking wine in their eldest brother's house:

chapters.zh: 有报信的来见约伯，说：牛正耕地，驴在旁边吃草，
chapters.en: And there came a messenger unto Job, and said, The oxen were plowing, and the asses feeding beside them:

chapters.zh: 示巴人忽然闯来，把牲畜掳去，并用刀杀了仆人；惟有我一人逃脱，来报信给你。」
chapters.en: And the Sabeans fell , and took them away; yea, they have slain the servants with the edge of the sword; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.

chapters.zh: 他还说话的时候，又有人来说：「神从天上降下火来，将群羊和仆人都烧灭了；惟有我一人逃脱，来报信给你。」
chapters.en: While he yet speaking, there came also another, and said, The fire of God is fallen from heaven, and hath burned up the sheep, and the servants, and consumed them; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.

chapters.zh: 他还说话的时候，又有人来说：「迦勒底人分作三队忽然闯来，把骆驼掳去，并用刀杀了仆人；惟有我一人逃脱，来报信给你。」
chapters.en: While he yet speaking, there came also another, and said, The Chaldeans made out three bands, and fell upon the camels, and have carried them away, yea, and slain the servants with the edge of the sword; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.

chapters.zh: 他还说话的时候，又有人来说：你的儿女正在他们长兄的家里吃饭喝酒，
chapters.en: While he yet speaking, there came also another, and said, Thy sons and thy daughters eating and drinking wine in their eldest brother's house:

chapters.zh: 不料，有狂风从旷野刮来，击打房屋的四角，房屋倒塌在少年人身上，他们就都死了；惟有我一人逃脱，来报信给你。」
chapters.en: And, behold, there came a great wind from the wilderness, and smote the four corners of the house, and it fell upon the young men, and they are dead; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.

chapters.zh: 约伯便起来，撕裂外袍，剃了头，伏在地上下拜，
chapters.en: Then Job arose, and rent his mantle, and shaved his head, and fell down upon the ground, and worshipped,

chapters.zh: 说：「我赤身出于母胎，也必赤身归回；赏赐的是耶和华，收取的也是耶和华。耶和华的名是应当称颂的。」
chapters.en: And said, Naked came I out of my mother's womb, and naked shall I return thither: the LORD gave, and the LORD hath taken away; blessed be the name of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 在这一切的事上约伯并不犯罪，也不以神为愚妄（或译：也不妄评神）。
chapters.en: In all this Job sinned not, nor charged God foolishly.

chapters.zh: 又有一天，神的众子来侍立在耶和华面前，撒但也来在其中。
chapters.en: Again there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD, and Satan came also among them to present himself before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华问撒但说：你从哪里来？撒但回答说：我从地上走来走去，往返而来。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Satan, From whence comest thou? And Satan answered the LORD, and said, From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.

chapters.zh: 耶和华问撒但说：你曾用心察看我的仆人约伯没有？地上再没有人像他完全正直，敬畏神，远离恶事。你虽激动我攻击他，无故地毁灭他，他仍然持守他的纯正。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that feareth God, and escheweth evil? and still he holdeth fast his integrity, although thou movedst me against him, to destroy him without cause.

chapters.zh: 撒但回答耶和华说：人以皮代皮，情愿舍去一切所有的，保全性命。
chapters.en: And Satan answered the LORD, and said, Skin for skin, yea, all that a man hath will he give for his life.

chapters.zh: 你且伸手伤他的骨头和他的肉，他必当面弃掉你。
chapters.en: But put forth thine hand now, and touch his bone and his flesh, and he will curse thee to thy face.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对撒但说：他在你手中，只要存留他的性命。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Satan, Behold, he in thine hand; but save his life.

chapters.zh: 于是撒但从耶和华面前退去，击打约伯，使他从脚掌到头顶长毒疮。
chapters.en: So went Satan forth from the presence of the LORD, and smote Job with sore boils from the sole of his foot unto his crown.

chapters.zh: 约伯就坐在炉灰中，拿瓦片刮身体。
chapters.en: And he took him a potsherd to scrape himself withal; and he sat down among the ashes.

chapters.zh: 他的妻子对他说：你仍然持守你的纯正么？你弃掉神，死了罢！
chapters.en: Then said his wife unto him, Dost thou still retain thine integrity? curse God, and die.

chapters.zh: 约伯却对他说：你说话像愚顽的妇一样。嗳！难道我们从神手里得福，不也受祸么？在这一切的事上约伯并不以口犯罪。
chapters.en: But he said unto her, Thou speakest as one of the foolish women speaketh. What? shall we receive good at the hand of God, and shall we not receive evil? In all this did not Job sin with his lips.

chapters.zh: 约伯的三个朋友 ─ 提幔人以利法、书亚人比勒达、拿玛人琐法 ─ 听说有这一切的灾祸临到他身上，各人就从本处约会同来，为他悲伤，安慰他。
chapters.en: Now when Job's three friends heard of all this evil that was come upon him, they came every one from his own place; Eliphaz the Temanite, and Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite: for they had made an appointment together to come to mourn with him and to comfort him.

chapters.zh: 他们远远地举目观看，认不出他来，就放声大哭。各人撕裂外袍，把尘土向天扬起来，落在自己的头上。
chapters.en: And when they lifted up their eyes afar off, and knew him not, they lifted up their voice, and wept; and they rent every one his mantle, and sprinkled dust upon their heads toward heaven.

chapters.zh: 他们就同他七天七夜坐在地上，一个人也不向他说句话，因为他极其痛苦。
chapters.en: So they sat down with him upon the ground seven days and seven nights, and none spake a word unto him: for they saw that grief was very great.

chapters.zh: 此后，约伯开口咒诅自己的生日，
chapters.en: After this opened Job his mouth, and cursed his day.

chapters.zh: 说：
chapters.en: And Job spake, and said,

chapters.zh: 愿我生的那日和说怀了男胎的那夜都灭没。
chapters.en: Let the day perish wherein I was born, and the night it was said, There is a man child conceived.

chapters.zh: 愿那日变为黑暗；愿神不从上面寻找他；愿亮光不照于其上。
chapters.en: Let that day be darkness; let not God regard it from above, neither let the light shine upon it.

chapters.zh: 愿黑暗和死荫索取那日；愿密云停在其上；愿日蚀恐吓他。
chapters.en: Let darkness and the shadow of death stain it; let a cloud dwell upon it; let the blackness of the day terrify it.

chapters.zh: 愿那夜被幽暗夺取，不在年中的日子同乐，也不入月中的数目。
chapters.en: As that night, let darkness seize upon it; let it not be joined unto the days of the year, let it not come into the number of the months.

chapters.zh: 愿那夜没有生育，其间也没有欢乐的声音。
chapters.en: Lo, let that night be solitary, let no joyful voice come therein.

chapters.zh: 愿那咒诅日子且能惹动鳄鱼的咒诅那夜。
chapters.en: Let them curse it that curse the day, who are ready to raise up their mourning.

chapters.zh: 愿那夜黎明的星宿变为黑暗，盼亮却不亮，也不见早晨的光线（原文是眼皮）；
chapters.en: Let the stars of the twilight thereof be dark; let it look for light, but none; neither let it see the dawning of the day:

chapters.zh: 因没有把怀我胎的门关闭，也没有将患难对我的眼隐藏。
chapters.en: Because it shut not up the doors of my womb, nor hid sorrow from mine eyes.

chapters.zh: 我为何不出母胎而死？为何不出母腹绝气？
chapters.en: Why died I not from the womb? did I give up the ghost when I came out of the belly?

chapters.zh: 为何有膝接收我？为何有奶哺养我？
chapters.en: Why did the knees prevent me? or why the breasts that I should suck?

chapters.zh: 不然，我就早已躺卧安睡，
chapters.en: For now should I have lain still and been quiet, I should have slept: then had I been at rest,

chapters.zh: 和地上为自己重造荒邱的君王、谋士，
chapters.en: With kings and counsellors of the earth, which built desolate places for themselves;

chapters.zh: 或与有金子、将银子装满了房屋的王子一同安息；
chapters.en: Or with princes that had gold, who filled their houses with silver:

chapters.zh: 或像隐而未现、不到期而落的胎，归于无有，如同未见光的婴孩。
chapters.en: Or as an hidden untimely birth I had not been; as infants never saw light.

chapters.zh: 在那里恶人止息搅扰，困乏人得享安息，
chapters.en: There the wicked cease troubling; and there the weary be at rest.

chapters.zh: 被囚的人同得安逸，不听见督工的声音。
chapters.en: the prisoners rest together; they hear not the voice of the oppressor.

chapters.zh: 大小都在那里；奴仆脱离主人的辖制。
chapters.en: The small and great are there; and the servant free from his master.

chapters.zh: 受患难的人为何有光赐给他呢？心中愁苦的人为何有生命赐给他呢？
chapters.en: Wherefore is light given to him that is in misery, and life unto the bitter soul;

chapters.zh: 他们切望死，却不得死；求死，胜于求隐藏的珍宝。
chapters.en: Which long for death, but it not; and dig for it more than for hid treasures;

chapters.zh: 他们寻见坟墓就快乐，极其欢喜。
chapters.en: Which rejoice exceedingly, are glad, when they can find the grave?

chapters.zh: 人的道路既然遮隐，神又把他四面围困，为何有光赐给他呢？
chapters.en: to a man whose way is hid, and whom God hath hedged in?

chapters.zh: 我未曾吃饭就发出叹息；我唉哼的声音涌出如水。
chapters.en: For my sighing cometh before I eat, and my roarings are poured out like the waters.

chapters.zh: 因我所恐惧的临到我身，我所惧怕的迎我而来。
chapters.en: For the thing which I greatly feared is come upon me, and that which I was afraid of is come unto me.

chapters.zh: 我不得安逸，不得平静，也不得安息，却有患难来到。
chapters.en: I was not in safety, neither had I rest, neither was I quiet; yet trouble came.

chapters.zh: 提幔人以利法回答说：
chapters.en: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,

chapters.zh: 人若想与你说话，你就厌烦么？但谁能忍住不说呢？
chapters.en: we assay to commune with thee, wilt thou be grieved? but who can withhold himself from speaking?

chapters.zh: 你素来教导许多的人，又坚固软弱的手。
chapters.en: Behold, thou hast instructed many, and thou hast strengthened the weak hands.

chapters.zh: 你的言语曾扶助那将要跌倒的人；你又使软弱的膝稳固。
chapters.en: Thy words have upholden him that was falling, and thou hast strengthened the feeble knees.

chapters.zh: 但现在祸患临到你，你就昏迷，挨近你，你便惊惶。
chapters.en: But now it is come upon thee, and thou faintest; it toucheth thee, and thou art troubled.

chapters.zh: 你的倚靠不是在你敬畏神么？你的盼望不是在你行事纯正么？
chapters.en: not thy fear, thy confidence, thy hope, and the uprightness of thy ways?

chapters.zh: 请你追想：无辜的人有谁灭亡？正直的人在何处剪除？
chapters.en: Remember, I pray thee, who perished, being innocent? or where were the righteous cut off?

chapters.zh: 按我所见，耕罪孽、种毒害的人都照样收割。
chapters.en: Even as I have seen, they that plow iniquity, and sow wickedness, reap the same.

chapters.zh: 神一出气，他们就灭亡；神一发怒，他们就消没。
chapters.en: By the blast of God they perish, and by the breath of his nostrils are they consumed.

chapters.zh: 狮子的吼叫和猛狮的声音尽都止息；少壮狮子的牙齿也都敲掉。
chapters.en: The roaring of the lion, and the voice of the fierce lion, and the teeth of the young lions, are broken.

chapters.zh: 老狮子因绝食而死；母狮之子也都离散。
chapters.en: The old lion perisheth for lack of prey, and the stout lion's whelps are scattered abroad.

chapters.zh: 我暗暗地得了默示；我耳朵也听其细微的声音。
chapters.en: Now a thing was secretly brought to me, and mine ear received a little thereof.

chapters.zh: 在思念夜中、异象之间，世人沉睡的时候，
chapters.en: In thoughts from the visions of the night, when deep sleep falleth on men,

chapters.zh: 恐惧、战兢临到我身，使我百骨打战。
chapters.en: Fear came upon me, and trembling, which made all my bones to shake.

chapters.zh: 有灵从我面前经过，我身上的毫毛直立。
chapters.en: Then a spirit passed before my face; the hair of my flesh stood up:

chapters.zh: 那灵停住，我却不能辨其形状；有影像在我眼前。我在静默中听见有声音说：
chapters.en: It stood still, but I could not discern the form thereof: an image before mine eyes, silence, and I heard a voice, ,

chapters.zh: 必死的人岂能比神公义么？人岂能比造他的主洁净么？
chapters.en: Shall mortal man be more just than God? shall a man be more pure than his maker?

chapters.zh: 主不信靠他的臣仆，并且指他的使者为愚昧；
chapters.en: Behold, he put no trust in his servants; and his angels he charged with folly:

chapters.zh: 何况那住在土房、根基在尘土里、被蠹虫所毁坏的人呢？
chapters.en: How much less them that dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation in the dust, are crushed before the moth?

chapters.zh: 早晚之间，就被毁灭，永归无有，无人理会。
chapters.en: They are destroyed from morning to evening: they perish for ever without any regarding .

chapters.zh: 他帐棚的绳索岂不从中抽出来呢？他死，且是无智慧而死。
chapters.en: Doth not their excellency in them go away? they die, even without wisdom.

chapters.zh: 你且呼求，有谁答应你？诸圣者之中，你转向哪一位呢？
chapters.en: Call now, if there be any that will answer thee; and to which of the saints wilt thou turn?

chapters.zh: 忿怒害死愚妄人；嫉妒杀死痴迷人。
chapters.en: For wrath killeth the foolish man, and envy slayeth the silly one.

chapters.zh: 我曾见愚妄人扎下根，但我忽然咒诅他的住处。
chapters.en: I have seen the foolish taking root: but suddenly I cursed his habitation.

chapters.zh: 他的儿女远离稳妥的地步，在城门口被压，并无人搭救。
chapters.en: His children are far from safety, and they are crushed in the gate, neither any to deliver .

chapters.zh: 他的庄稼有饥饿的人吃尽了，就是在荆棘里的也抢去了；他的财宝有网罗张口吞灭了。
chapters.en: Whose harvest the hungry eateth up, and taketh it even out of the thorns, and the robber swalloweth up their substance.

chapters.zh: 祸患原不是从土中出来；患难也不是从地里发生。
chapters.en: Although affliction cometh not forth of the dust, neither doth trouble spring out of the ground;

chapters.zh: 人生在世必遇患难，如同火星飞腾。
chapters.en: Yet man is born unto trouble, as the sparks fly upward.

chapters.zh: 至于我，我必仰望神，把我的事情托付他。
chapters.en: I would seek unto God, and unto God would I commit my cause:

chapters.zh: 他行大事不可测度，行奇事不可胜数：
chapters.en: Which doeth great things and unsearchable; marvellous things without number:

chapters.zh: 降雨在地上，赐水于田里；
chapters.en: Who giveth rain upon the earth, and sendeth waters upon the fields:

chapters.zh: 将卑微的安置在高处，将哀痛的举到稳妥之地；
chapters.en: To set up on high those that be low; that those which mourn may be exalted to safety.

chapters.zh: 破坏狡猾人的计谋，使他们所谋的不得成就。
chapters.en: He disappointeth the devices of the crafty, so that their hands cannot perform enterprise.

chapters.zh: 他叫有智慧的中了自己的诡计，使狡诈人的计谋速速灭亡。
chapters.en: He taketh the wise in their own craftiness: and the counsel of the froward is carried headlong.

chapters.zh: 他们白昼遇见黑暗，午间摸索如在夜间。
chapters.en: They meet with darkness in the daytime, and grope in the noonday as in the night.

chapters.zh: 神拯救穷乏人脱离他们口中的刀和强暴人的手。
chapters.en: But he saveth the poor from the sword, from their mouth, and from the hand of the mighty.

chapters.zh: 这样，贫寒的人有指望，罪孽之辈必塞口无言。
chapters.en: So the poor hath hope, and iniquity stoppeth her mouth.

chapters.zh: 神所惩治的人是有福的！所以你不可轻看全能者的管教。
chapters.en: Behold, happy the man whom God correcteth: therefore despise not thou the chastening of the Almighty:

chapters.zh: 因为他打破，又缠裹；他击伤，用手医治。
chapters.en: For he maketh sore, and bindeth up: he woundeth, and his hands make whole.

chapters.zh: 你六次遭难，他必救你；就是七次，灾祸也无法害你。
chapters.en: He shall deliver thee in six troubles: yea, in seven there shall no evil touch thee.

chapters.zh: 在饥荒中，他必救你脱离死亡；在争战中，他必救你脱离刀剑的权力。
chapters.en: In famine he shall redeem thee from death: and in war from the power of the sword.

chapters.zh: 你必被隐藏，不受口舌之害；灾殃临到，你也不惧怕。
chapters.en: Thou shalt be hid from the scourge of the tongue: neither shalt thou be afraid of destruction when it cometh.

chapters.zh: 你遇见灾害饥馑，就必嬉笑；地上的野兽，你也不惧怕。
chapters.en: At destruction and famine thou shalt laugh: neither shalt thou be afraid of the beasts of the earth.

chapters.zh: 因为你必与田间的石头立约；田里的野兽也必与你和好。
chapters.en: For thou shalt be in league with the stones of the field: and the beasts of the field shall be at peace with thee.

chapters.zh: 你必知道你帐棚平安，要查看你的羊圈，一无所失；
chapters.en: And thou shalt know that thy tabernacle in peace; and thou shalt visit thy habitation, and shalt not sin.

chapters.zh: 也必知道你的后裔将来发达，你的子孙像地上的青草。
chapters.en: Thou shalt know also that thy seed great, and thine offspring as the grass of the earth.

chapters.zh: 你必寿高年迈才归坟墓，好像禾捆到时收藏。
chapters.en: Thou shalt come to grave in a full age, like as a shock of corn cometh in in his season.

chapters.zh: 这理，我们已经考察，本是如此。你须要听，要知道是与自己有益。
chapters.en: Lo this, we have searched it, so it ; hear it, and know thou for thy good.

chapters.zh: 约伯回答说：
chapters.en: But Job answered and said,

chapters.zh: 惟愿我的烦恼称一称，我一切的灾害放在天平里；
chapters.en: Oh that my grief were throughly weighed, and my calamity laid in the balances together!

chapters.zh: 现今都比海沙更重，所以我的言语急躁。
chapters.en: For now it would be heavier than the sand of the sea: therefore my words are swallowed up.

chapters.zh: 因全能者的箭射入我身；其毒，我的灵喝尽了；神的惊吓摆阵攻击我。
chapters.en: For the arrows of the Almighty within me, the poison whereof drinketh up my spirit: the terrors of God do set themselves in array against me.

chapters.zh: 野驴有草岂能叫唤？牛有料岂能吼叫？
chapters.en: Doth the wild ass bray when he hath grass? or loweth the ox over his fodder?

chapters.zh: 物淡而无盐岂可吃么？蛋青有甚么滋味呢？
chapters.en: Can that which is unsavoury be eaten without salt? or is there taste in the white of an egg?

chapters.zh: 看为可厌的食物，我心不肯挨近。
chapters.en: The things my soul refused to touch as my sorrowful meat.

chapters.zh: 惟愿我得著所求的，愿神赐我所切望的 !
chapters.en: Oh that I might have my request; and that God would grant the thing that I long for!

chapters.zh: 就是愿神把我压碎，伸手将我剪除。
chapters.en: Even that it would please God to destroy me; that he would let loose his hand, and cut me off!

chapters.zh: 我因没有违弃那圣者的言语，就仍以此为安慰，在不止息的痛苦中还可踊跃。
chapters.en: Then should I yet have comfort; yea, I would harden myself in sorrow: let him not spare; for I have not concealed the words of the Holy One.

chapters.zh: 我有甚么气力使我等候？我有甚么结局使我忍耐？
chapters.en: What my strength, that I should hope? and what mine end, that I should prolong my life?

chapters.zh: 我的气力岂是石头的气力？我的肉身岂是铜的呢？
chapters.en: my strength the strength of stones? or my flesh of brass?

chapters.zh: 在我岂不是毫无帮助么？智慧岂不是从我心中赶出净尽么？
chapters.en: not my help in me? and is wisdom driven quite from me?

chapters.zh: 那将要灰心、离弃全能者、不敬畏神的人，他的朋友当以慈爱待他。
chapters.en: To him that is afflicted pity from his friend; but he forsaketh the fear of the Almighty.

chapters.zh: 我的弟兄诡诈，好像溪水，又像溪水流干的河道。
chapters.en: My brethren have dealt deceitfully as a brook, as the stream of brooks they pass away;

chapters.zh: 这河因结冰发黑，有雪藏在其中；
chapters.en: Which are blackish by reason of the ice, wherein the snow is hid:

chapters.zh: 天气渐暖就随时消化，日头炎热便从原处干涸。
chapters.en: What time they wax warm, they vanish: when it is hot, they are consumed out of their place.

chapters.zh: 结伴的客旅离弃大道，顺河偏行，到荒野之地死亡。
chapters.en: The paths of their way are turned aside; they go to nothing, and perish.

chapters.zh: 提玛结伴的客旅瞻望；示巴同伙的人等候。
chapters.en: The troops of Tema looked, the companies of Sheba waited for them.

chapters.zh: 他们因失了盼望就抱愧，来到那里便蒙羞。
chapters.en: They were confounded because they had hoped; they came thither, and were ashamed.

chapters.zh: 现在你们正是这样，看见惊吓的事便惧怕。
chapters.en: For now ye are nothing; ye see casting down, and are afraid.

chapters.zh: 我岂说：请你们供给我，从你们的财物中送礼物给我？
chapters.en: Did I say, Bring unto me? or, Give a reward for me of your substance?

chapters.zh: 岂说：拯救我脱离敌人的手么？救赎我脱离强暴人的手么？
chapters.en: Or, Deliver me from the enemy's hand? or, Redeem me from the hand of the mighty?

chapters.zh: 请你们教导我，我便不作声；使我明白在何事上有错。
chapters.en: Teach me, and I will hold my tongue: and cause me to understand wherein I have erred.

chapters.zh: 正直的言语力量何其大！但你们责备是责备甚么呢？
chapters.en: How forcible are right words! but what doth your arguing reprove?

chapters.zh: 绝望人的讲论既然如风，你们还想要驳正言语么？
chapters.en: Do ye imagine to reprove words, and the speeches of one that is desperate, as wind?

chapters.zh: 你们想为孤儿拈阄，以朋友当货物。
chapters.en: Yea, ye overwhelm the fatherless, and ye dig for your friend.

chapters.zh: 现在请你们看看我，我决不当面说谎。
chapters.en: Now therefore be content, look upon me; for evident unto you if I lie.

chapters.zh: 请你们转意，不要不公；请再转意，我的事有理。
chapters.en: Return, I pray you, let it not be iniquity; yea, return again, my righteousness in it.

chapters.zh: 我的舌上岂有不义么？我的口里岂不辨奸恶么？
chapters.en: Is there iniquity in my tongue? cannot my taste discern perverse things?

chapters.zh: 人在世上岂无争战么？他的日子不像雇工人的日子么？
chapters.en: not an appointed time to man upon earth? his days also like the days of an hireling?

chapters.zh: 像奴仆切慕黑影，像雇工人盼望工价；
chapters.en: As a servant earnestly desireth the shadow, and as an hireling looketh for his work:

chapters.zh: 我也照样经过困苦的日月，夜间的疲乏为我而定。
chapters.en: So am I made to possess months of vanity, and wearisome nights are appointed to me.

chapters.zh: 我躺卧的时候便说：我何时起来，黑夜就过去呢？我尽是反来覆去，直到天亮。
chapters.en: When I lie down, I say, When shall I arise, and the night be gone? and I am full of tossings to and fro unto the dawning of the day.

chapters.zh: 我的肉体以虫子和尘土为衣；我的皮肤才收了口又重新破裂。
chapters.en: My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust; my skin is broken, and become loathsome.

chapters.zh: 我的日子比梭更快，都消耗在无指望之中。
chapters.en: My days are swifter than a weaver's shuttle, and are spent without hope.

chapters.zh: 求你想念，我的生命不过是一口气；我的眼睛必不再见福乐。
chapters.en: O remember that my life wind: mine eye shall no more see good.

chapters.zh: 观看我的人，他的眼必不再见我；你的眼目要看我，我却不在了。
chapters.en: The eye of him that hath seen me shall see me no : thine eyes upon me, and I not.

chapters.zh: 云彩消散而过；照样，人下阴间也不再上来。
chapters.en: the cloud is consumed and vanisheth away: so he that goeth down to the grave shall come up no .

chapters.zh: 他不再回自己的家；故土也不再认识他。
chapters.en: He shall return no more to his house, neither shall his place know him any more.

chapters.zh: 我不禁止我口；我灵愁苦，要发出言语；我心苦恼，要吐露哀情。
chapters.en: Therefore I will not refrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.

chapters.zh: 我对神说：我岂是洋海，岂是大鱼，你竟防守我呢？
chapters.en: I a sea, or a whale, that thou settest a watch over me?

chapters.zh: 若说：我的床必安慰我，我的榻必解释我的苦情，
chapters.en: When I say, My bed shall comfort me, my couch shall ease my complaint;

chapters.zh: 你就用梦惊骇我，用异象恐吓我，
chapters.en: Then thou scarest me with dreams, and terrifiest me through visions:

chapters.zh: 甚至我宁肯噎死，宁肯死亡，胜似留我这一身的骨头。
chapters.en: So that my soul chooseth strangling, death rather than my life.

chapters.zh: 我厌弃性命，不愿永活。你任凭我罢，因我的日子都是虚空。
chapters.en: I loathe ; I would not live alway: let me alone; for my days vanity.

chapters.zh: 人算甚么，你竟看他为大，将他放在心上？
chapters.en: What man, that thou shouldest magnify him? and that thou shouldest set thine heart upon him?

chapters.zh: 每早鉴察他，时刻试验他？
chapters.en: And thou shouldest visit him every morning, try him every moment?

chapters.zh: 你到何时才转眼不看我，才任凭我咽下唾沫呢？
chapters.en: How long wilt thou not depart from me, nor let me alone till I swallow down my spittle?

chapters.zh: 鉴察人的主啊，我若有罪，于你何妨？为何以我当你的箭靶子，使我厌弃自己的性命？
chapters.en: I have sinned; what shall I do unto thee, O thou preserver of men? why hast thou set me as a mark against thee, so that I am a burden to myself?

chapters.zh: 为何不赦免我的过犯，除掉我的罪孽？我现今要躺卧在尘土中；你要殷勤地寻找我，我却不在了。
chapters.en: And why dost thou not pardon my transgression, and take away mine iniquity? for now shall I sleep in the dust; and thou shalt seek me in the morning, but I not .

chapters.zh: 书亚人比勒达回答说：
chapters.en: Then answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,

chapters.zh: 这些话你要说到几时？口中的言语如狂风要到几时呢？
chapters.en: How long wilt thou speak these ? and the words of thy mouth a strong wind?

chapters.zh: 神岂能偏离公平？全能者岂能偏离公义？
chapters.en: Doth God pervert judgment? or doth the Almighty pervert justice?

chapters.zh: 或者你的儿女得罪了他；他使他们受报应。
chapters.en: If thy children have sinned against him, and he have cast them away for their transgression;

chapters.zh: 你若殷勤地寻求神，向全能者恳求；
chapters.en: If thou wouldest seek unto God betimes, and make thy supplication to the Almighty;

chapters.zh: 你若清洁正直，他必定为你起来，使你公义的居所兴旺。
chapters.en: If thou pure and upright; surely now he would awake for thee, and make the habitation of thy righteousness prosperous.

chapters.zh: 你起初虽然微小，终久必甚发达。
chapters.en: Though thy beginning was small, yet thy latter end should greatly increase.

chapters.zh: 请你考问前代，追念他们的列祖所查究的。
chapters.en: For enquire, I pray thee, of the former age, and prepare thyself to the search of their fathers:

chapters.zh: 我们不过从昨日才有，一无所知；我们在世的日子好像影儿。
chapters.en: (For we yesterday, and know nothing, because our days upon earth a shadow:)

chapters.zh: 他们岂不指教你，告诉你，从心里发出言语来呢？
chapters.en: Shall not they teach thee, tell thee, and utter words out of their heart?

chapters.zh: 蒲草没有泥岂能发长？芦荻没有水岂能生发？
chapters.en: Can the rush grow up without mire? can the flag grow without water?

chapters.zh: 尚青的时候，还没有割下，比百样的草先枯槁。
chapters.en: Whilst it yet in his greenness, not cut down, it withereth before any herb.

chapters.zh: 凡忘记神的人，景况也是这样；不虔敬人的指望要灭没。
chapters.en: So the paths of all that forget God; and the hypocrite's hope shall perish:

chapters.zh: 他所仰赖的必折断；他所倚靠的是蜘蛛网。
chapters.en: Whose hope shall be cut off, and whose trust a spider's web.

chapters.zh: 他要倚靠房屋，房屋却站立不住；他要抓住房屋，房屋却不能存留。
chapters.en: He shall lean upon his house, but it shall not stand: he shall hold it fast, but it shall not endure.

chapters.zh: 他在日光之下发青，蔓子爬满了园子；
chapters.en: He green before the sun, and his branch shooteth forth in his garden.

chapters.zh: 他的根盘绕石堆，扎入石地。
chapters.en: His roots are wrapped about the heap, seeth the place of stones.

chapters.zh: 他若从本地被拔出，那地就不认识他，说：我没有见过你。
chapters.en: If he destroy him from his place, then shall deny him, , I have not seen thee.

chapters.zh: 看哪，这就是他道中之乐；以后必另有人从地而生。
chapters.en: Behold, this the joy of his way, and out of the earth shall others grow.

chapters.zh: 神必不丢弃完全人，也不扶助邪恶人。
chapters.en: Behold, God will not cast away a perfect , neither will he help the evil doers:

chapters.zh: 他还要以喜笑充满你的口，以欢呼充满你的嘴。
chapters.en: Till he fill thy mouth with laughing, and thy lips with rejoicing.

chapters.zh: 恨恶你的要披戴惭愧；恶人的帐棚必归于无有。
chapters.en: They that hate thee shall be clothed with shame; and the dwelling place of the wicked shall come to nought.

chapters.zh: 约伯回答说：
chapters.en: Then Job answered and said,

chapters.zh: 我真知道是这样；但人在神面前怎能成为义呢？
chapters.en: I know so of a truth: but how should man be just with God?

chapters.zh: 若愿意与他争辩，千中之一也不能回答。
chapters.en: If he will contend with him, he cannot answer him one of a thousand.

chapters.zh: 他心里有智慧，且大有能力。谁向神刚硬而得亨通呢？
chapters.en: wise in heart, and mighty in strength: who hath hardened against him, and hath prospered?

chapters.zh: 他发怒，把山翻倒挪移，山并不知觉。
chapters.en: Which removeth the mountains, and they know not: which overturneth them in his anger.

chapters.zh: 他使地震动，离其本位，地的柱子就摇撼。
chapters.en: Which shaketh the earth out of her place, and the pillars thereof tremble.

chapters.zh: 他吩咐日头不出来，就不出来，又封闭众星。
chapters.en: Which commandeth the sun, and it riseth not; and sealeth up the stars.

chapters.zh: 他独自铺张苍天，步行在海浪之上。
chapters.en: Which alone spreadeth out the heavens, and treadeth upon the waves of the sea.

chapters.zh: 他造北斗、参星、昴星，并南方的密宫；
chapters.en: Which maketh Arcturus, Orion, and Pleiades, and the chambers of the south.

chapters.zh: 他行大事，不可测度，行奇事，不可胜数。
chapters.en: Which doeth great things past finding out; yea, and wonders without number.

chapters.zh: 他从我旁边经过，我却不看见；他在我面前行走，我倒不知觉。
chapters.en: Lo, he goeth by me, and I see not: he passeth on also, but I perceive him not.

chapters.zh: 他夺取，谁能阻挡？谁敢问他：你做甚么？
chapters.en: Behold, he taketh away, who can hinder him? who will say unto him, What doest thou?

chapters.zh: 神必不收回他的怒气；扶助拉哈伯的，屈身在他以下。
chapters.en: God will not withdraw his anger, the proud helpers do stoop under him.

chapters.zh: 既是这样，我怎敢回答他，怎敢选择言语与他辩论呢？
chapters.en: How much less shall I answer him, choose out my words with him?

chapters.zh: 我虽有义，也不回答他，只要向那审判我的恳求。
chapters.en: Whom, though I were righteous, would I not answer, I would make supplication to my judge.

chapters.zh: 我若呼吁，他应允我；我仍不信他真听我的声音。
chapters.en: If I had called, and he had answered me; would I not believe that he had hearkened unto my voice.

chapters.zh: 他用暴风折断我，无故地加增我的损伤。
chapters.en: For he breaketh me with a tempest, and multiplieth my wounds without cause.

chapters.zh: 我就是喘一口气，他都不容，倒使我满心苦恼。
chapters.en: He will not suffer me to take my breath, but filleth me with bitterness.

chapters.zh: 若论力量，他真有能力！若论审判，他说谁能将我传来呢？
chapters.en: If of strength, lo, strong: and if of judgment, who shall set me a time ?

chapters.zh: 我虽有义，自己的口要定我为有罪；我虽完全，我口必显我为弯曲。
chapters.en: If I justify myself, mine own mouth shall condemn me: , I perfect, it shall also prove me perverse.

chapters.zh: 我本完全，不顾自己；我厌恶我的性命。
chapters.en: I perfect, would I not know my soul: I would despise my life.

chapters.zh: 善恶无分，都是一样；所以我说，完全人和恶人，他都灭绝。
chapters.en: This one , therefore I said , He destroyeth the perfect and the wicked.

chapters.zh: 若忽然遭杀害之祸，他必戏笑无辜的人遇难。
chapters.en: If the scourge slay suddenly, he will laugh at the trial of the innocent.

chapters.zh: 世界交在恶人手中；蒙蔽世界审判官的脸，若不是他，是谁呢？
chapters.en: The earth is given into the hand of the wicked: he covereth the faces of the judges thereof; if not, where, who he?

chapters.zh: 我的日子比跑信的更快，急速过去，不见福乐。
chapters.en: Now my days are swifter than a post: they flee away, they see no good.

chapters.zh: 我的日子过去如快船，如急落抓食的鹰。
chapters.en: They are passed away as the swift ships: as the eagle hasteth to the prey.

chapters.zh: 我若说：我要忘记我的哀情，除去我的愁容，心中畅快；
chapters.en: If I say, I will forget my complaint, I will leave off my heaviness, and comfort :

chapters.zh: 我因愁苦而惧怕，知道你必不以我为无辜。
chapters.en: I am afraid of all my sorrows, I know that thou wilt not hold me innocent.

chapters.zh: 我必被你定为有罪，我何必徒然劳苦呢？
chapters.en: I be wicked, why then labour I in vain?

chapters.zh: 我若用雪水洗身，用碱洁净我的手，
chapters.en: If I wash myself with snow water, and make my hands never so clean;

chapters.zh: 你还要扔我在坑里，我的衣服都憎恶我。
chapters.en: Yet shalt thou plunge me in the ditch, and mine own clothes shall abhor me.

chapters.zh: 他本不像我是人，使我可以回答他，又使我们可以同听审判。
chapters.en: For not a man, as I I should answer him, we should come together in judgment.

chapters.zh: 我们中间没有听讼的人可以向我们两造按手。
chapters.en: Neither is there any daysman betwixt us, might lay his hand upon us both.

chapters.zh: 愿他把杖离开我，不使惊惶威吓我。
chapters.en: Let him take his rod away from me, and let not his fear terrify me:

chapters.zh: 我就说话，也不惧怕他，现在我却不是那样。
chapters.en: would I speak, and not fear him; but not so with me.

chapters.zh: 我厌烦我的性命，必由著自己述说我的哀情；因心里苦恼，我要说话，
chapters.en: My soul is weary of my life; I will leave my complaint upon myself; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.

chapters.zh: 对神说：不要定我有罪，要指示我，你为何与我争辩？
chapters.en: I will say unto God, Do not condemn me; shew me wherefore thou contendest with me.

chapters.zh: 你手所造的，你又欺压，又藐视，却光照恶人的计谋。这事你以为美么？
chapters.en: good unto thee that thou shouldest oppress, that thou shouldest despise the work of thine hands, and shine upon the counsel of the wicked?

chapters.zh: 你的眼岂是肉眼？你查看岂像人查看么？
chapters.en: Hast thou eyes of flesh? or seest thou as man seeth?

chapters.zh: 你的日子岂像人的日子，你的年岁岂像人的年岁，
chapters.en: thy days as the days of man? thy years as man's days,

chapters.zh: 就追问我的罪孽，寻察我的罪过么？
chapters.en: That thou enquirest after mine iniquity, and searchest after my sin?

chapters.zh: 其实，你知道我没有罪恶，并没有能救我脱离你手的。
chapters.en: Thou knowest that I am not wicked; and none that can deliver out of thine hand.

chapters.zh: 你的手创造我，造就我的四肢百体，你还要毁灭我。
chapters.en: Thine hands have made me and fashioned me together round about; yet thou dost destroy me.

chapters.zh: 求你记念 ─ 制造我如抟泥一般，你还要使我归于尘土么？
chapters.en: Remember, I beseech thee, that thou hast made me as the clay; and wilt thou bring me into dust again?

chapters.zh: 你不是倒出我来好像奶，使我凝结如同奶饼么？
chapters.en: Hast thou not poured me out as milk, and curdled me like cheese?

chapters.zh: 你以皮和肉为衣给我穿上，用骨与筋把我全体联络。
chapters.en: Thou hast clothed me with skin and flesh, and hast fenced me with bones and sinews.

chapters.zh: 你将生命和慈爱赐给我；你也眷顾保全我的心灵。
chapters.en: Thou hast granted me life and favour, and thy visitation hath preserved my spirit.

chapters.zh: 然而，你待我的这些事早已藏在你心里；我知道你久有此意。
chapters.en: And these hast thou hid in thine heart: I know that this with thee.

chapters.zh: 我若犯罪，你就察看我，并不赦免我的罪孽。
chapters.en: If I sin, then thou markest me, and thou wilt not acquit me from mine iniquity.

chapters.zh: 我若行恶，便有了祸；我若为义，也不敢抬头，正是满心羞愧，眼见我的苦情。
chapters.en: If I be wicked, woe unto me; and I be righteous, will I not lift up my head. full of confusion; therefore see thou mine affliction;

chapters.zh: 我若昂首自得，你就追捕我如狮子，又在我身上显出奇能。
chapters.en: For it increaseth. Thou huntest me as a fierce lion: and again thou shewest thyself marvellous upon me.

chapters.zh: 你重立见证攻击我，向我加增恼怒，如军兵更换著攻击我。
chapters.en: Thou renewest thy witnesses against me, and increasest thine indignation upon me; changes and war against me.

chapters.zh: 你为何使我出母胎呢？不如我当时气绝，无人得见我；
chapters.en: Wherefore then hast thou brought me forth out of the womb? Oh that I had given up the ghost, and no eye had seen me!

chapters.zh: 这样，就如没有我一般，一出母胎就被送入坟墓。
chapters.en: I should have been as though I had not been; I should have been carried from the womb to the grave.

chapters.zh: 我的日子不是甚少么？求你停手宽容我，叫我在往而不返之先 ─ 就是往黑暗和死荫之地以先 ─ 可以稍得畅快。
chapters.en: not my days few? cease let me alone, that I may take comfort a little,

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: Before I go I shall not return, to the land of darkness and the shadow of death;

chapters.zh: 那地甚是幽暗，是死荫混沌之地；那里的光好像幽暗。
chapters.en: A land of darkness, as darkness of the shadow of death, without any order, and the light as darkness.

chapters.zh: 拿玛人琐法回答说：
chapters.en: Then answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said,

chapters.zh: 这许多的言语岂不该回答么？多嘴多舌的人岂可称为义么？
chapters.en: Should not the multitude of words be answered? and should a man full of talk be justified?

chapters.zh: 你夸大的话岂能使人不作声么？你戏笑的时候岂没有人叫你害羞么？
chapters.en: Should thy lies make men hold their peace? and when thou mockest, shall no man make thee ashamed?

chapters.zh: 你说：我的道理纯全；我在你眼前洁净。
chapters.en: For thou hast said, My doctrine pure, and I am clean in thine eyes.

chapters.zh: 惟愿神说话；愿他开口攻击你，
chapters.en: But oh that God would speak, and open his lips against thee;

chapters.zh: 并将智慧的奥秘指示你；他有诸般的智识。所以当知道神追讨你比你罪孽该得的还少。
chapters.en: And that he would shew thee the secrets of wisdom, that double to that which is! Know therefore that God exacteth of thee than thine iniquity .

chapters.zh: 你考察就能测透神吗？你岂能尽情测透全能者吗？
chapters.en: Canst thou by searching find out God? canst thou find out the Almighty unto perfection?

chapters.zh: 他的智慧高于天，你还能做甚么？深于阴间，你还能知道甚么？
chapters.en: as high as heaven; what canst thou do? deeper than hell; what canst thou know?

chapters.zh: 其量比地长，比海宽。
chapters.en: The measure thereof longer than the earth, and broader than the sea.

chapters.zh: 他若经过，将人拘禁，招人受审，谁能阻挡他呢？
chapters.en: If he cut off, and shut up, or gather together, then who can hinder him?

chapters.zh: 他本知道虚妄的人；人的罪孽，他虽不留意，还是无所不见。
chapters.en: For he knoweth vain men: he seeth wickedness also; will he not then consider ?

chapters.zh: 空虚的人却毫无知识；人生在世好像野驴的驹子。
chapters.en: For vain man would be wise, though man be born a wild ass's colt.

chapters.zh: 你若将心安正，又向主举手；
chapters.en: If thou prepare thine heart, and stretch out thine hands toward him;

chapters.zh: 你手里若有罪孽，就当远远地除掉，也不容非义住在你帐棚之中。
chapters.en: If iniquity in thine hand, put it far away, and let not wickedness dwell in thy tabernacles.

chapters.zh: 那时，你必仰起脸来毫无斑点；你也必坚固，无所惧怕。
chapters.en: For then shalt thou lift up thy face without spot; yea, thou shalt be stedfast, and shalt not fear:

chapters.zh: 你必忘记你的苦楚，就是想起也如流过去的水一样。
chapters.en: Because thou shalt forget misery, remember as waters pass away:

chapters.zh: 你在世的日子要比正午更明，虽有黑暗仍像早晨。
chapters.en: And age shall be clearer than the noonday; thou shalt shine forth, thou shalt be as the morning.

chapters.zh: 你因有指望就必稳固，也必四围巡查，坦然安息。
chapters.en: And thou shalt be secure, because there is hope; yea, thou shalt dig thou shalt take thy rest in safety.

chapters.zh: 你躺卧，无人惊吓，且有许多人向你求恩。
chapters.en: Also thou shalt lie down, and none shall make afraid; yea, many shall make suit unto thee.

chapters.zh: 但恶人的眼目必要失明。他们无路可逃；他们的指望就是气绝。
chapters.en: But the eyes of the wicked shall fail, and they shall not escape, and their hope the giving up of the ghost.

chapters.zh: 约伯回答说：
chapters.en: And Job answered and said,

chapters.zh: 你们真是子民哪，你们死亡，智慧也就灭没了。
chapters.en: No doubt but ye the people, and wisdom shall die with you.

chapters.zh: 但我也有聪明，与你们一样，并非不及你们。你们所说的，谁不知道呢？
chapters.en: But I have understanding as well as you; I not inferior to you: yea, who knoweth not such things as these?

chapters.zh: 我这求告神、蒙他应允的人竟成了朋友所讥笑的；公义完全人竟受了人的讥笑。
chapters.en: I am one mocked of his neighbour, who calleth upon God, and he answereth him: the just upright laughed to scorn.

chapters.zh: 安逸的人心里藐视灾祸；这灾祸常常等待滑脚的人。
chapters.en: He that is ready to slip with feet a lamp despised in the thought of him that is at ease.

chapters.zh: 强盗的帐棚兴旺，惹神的人稳固，神多将财物送到他们手中。
chapters.en: The tabernacles of robbers prosper, and they that provoke God are secure; into whose hand God bringeth .

chapters.zh: 你且问走兽，走兽必指教你；又问空中的飞鸟，飞鸟必告诉你；
chapters.en: But ask now the beasts, and they shall teach thee; and the fowls of the air, and they shall tell thee:

chapters.zh: 或与地说话，地必指教你；海中的鱼也必向你说明。
chapters.en: Or speak to the earth, and it shall teach thee: and the fishes of the sea shall declare unto thee.

chapters.zh: 看这一切，谁不知道是耶和华的手做成的呢？
chapters.en: Who knoweth not in all these that the hand of the LORD hath wrought this?

chapters.zh: 凡活物的生命和人类的气息都在他手中。
chapters.en: In whose hand the soul of every living thing, and the breath of all mankind.

chapters.zh: 耳朵岂不试验言语，正如上膛尝食物么？
chapters.en: Doth not the ear try words? and the mouth taste his meat?

chapters.zh: 年老的有智慧；寿高的有知识。
chapters.en: With the ancient wisdom; and in length of days understanding.

chapters.zh: 在神有智慧和能力；他有谋略和知识。
chapters.en: With him wisdom and strength, he hath counsel and understanding.

chapters.zh: 他拆毁的，就不能再建造；他捆住人，便不得开释。
chapters.en: Behold, he breaketh down, and it cannot be built again: he shutteth up a man, and there can be no opening.

chapters.zh: 他把水留住，水便枯干；他再发出水来，水就翻地。
chapters.en: Behold, he withholdeth the waters, and they dry up: also he sendeth them out, and they overturn the earth.

chapters.zh: 在他有能力和智慧，被诱惑的与诱惑人的都是属他。
chapters.en: With him strength and wisdom: the deceived and the deceiver his.

chapters.zh: 他把谋士剥衣掳去，又使审判官变成愚人。
chapters.en: He leadeth counsellors away spoiled, and maketh the judges fools.

chapters.zh: 他放松君王的绑，又用带子捆他们的腰。
chapters.en: He looseth the bond of kings, and girdeth their loins with a girdle.

chapters.zh: 他把祭司剥衣掳去，又使有能的人倾败。
chapters.en: He leadeth princes away spoiled, and overthroweth the mighty.

chapters.zh: 他废去忠信人的讲论，又夺去老人的聪明。
chapters.en: He removeth away the speech of the trusty, and taketh away the understanding of the aged.

chapters.zh: 他使君王蒙羞被辱，放松有力之人的腰带。
chapters.en: He poureth contempt upon princes, and weakeneth the strength of the mighty.

chapters.zh: 他将深奥的事从黑暗中彰显，使死荫显为光明。
chapters.en: He discovereth deep things out of darkness, and bringeth out to light the shadow of death.

chapters.zh: 他使邦国兴旺而又毁灭；他使邦国开广而又掳去。
chapters.en: He increaseth the nations, and destroyeth them: he enlargeth the nations, and straiteneth them .

chapters.zh: 他将地上民中首领的聪明夺去，使他们在荒废无路之地漂流；
chapters.en: He taketh away the heart of the chief of the people of the earth, and causeth them to wander in a wilderness no way.

chapters.zh: 他们无光，在黑暗中摸索，又使他们东倒西歪，像醉酒的人一样。
chapters.en: They grope in the dark without light, and he maketh them to stagger like drunken .

chapters.zh: 这一切，我眼都见过；我耳都听过，而且明白。
chapters.en: Lo, mine eye hath seen all , mine ear hath heard and understood it.

chapters.zh: 你们所知道的，我也知道，并非不及你们。
chapters.en: What ye know, do I know also: I not inferior unto you.

chapters.zh: 我真要对全能者说话；我愿与神理论。
chapters.en: Surely I would speak to the Almighty, and I desire to reason with God.

chapters.zh: 你们是编造谎言的，都是无用的医生。
chapters.en: But ye forgers of lies, ye all physicians of no value.

chapters.zh: 惟愿你们全然不作声；这就算为你们的智慧！
chapters.en: O that ye would altogether hold your peace! and it should be your wisdom.

chapters.zh: 请你们听我的辩论，留心听我口中的分诉。
chapters.en: Hear now my reasoning, and hearken to the pleadings of my lips.

chapters.zh: 你们要为神说不义的话么？为他说诡诈的言语么？
chapters.en: Will ye speak wickedly for God? and talk deceitfully for him?

chapters.zh: 你们要为神徇情么？要为他争论么？
chapters.en: Will ye accept his person? will ye contend for God?

chapters.zh: 他查出你们来，这岂是好么？人欺哄人，你们也要照样欺哄他么？
chapters.en: Is it good that he should search you out? or as one man mocketh another, do ye mock him?

chapters.zh: 你们若暗中徇情，他必要责备你们。
chapters.en: He will surely reprove you, if ye do secretly accept persons.

chapters.zh: 他的尊荣岂不叫你们惧怕么？他的惊吓岂不临到你们么？
chapters.en: Shall not his excellency make you afraid? and his dread fall upon you?

chapters.zh: 你们以为可记念的箴言是炉灰的箴言；你们以为可靠的坚垒是淤泥的坚垒。
chapters.en: Your remembrances like unto ashes, your bodies to bodies of clay.

chapters.zh: 你们不要作声，任凭我罢！让我说话，无论如何我都承当。
chapters.en: Hold your peace, let me alone, that I may speak, and let come on me what .

chapters.zh: 我何必把我的肉挂在牙上，将我的命放在手中。
chapters.en: Wherefore do I take my flesh in my teeth, and put my life in mine hand?

chapters.zh: 他必杀我；我虽无指望，然而我在他面前还要辩明我所行的。
chapters.en: Though he slay me, yet will I trust in him: but I will maintain mine own ways before him.

chapters.zh: 这要成为我的拯救，因为不虔诚的人不得到他面前。
chapters.en: He also my salvation: for an hypocrite shall not come before him.

chapters.zh: 你们要细听我的言语，使我所辩论的入你们的耳中。
chapters.en: Hear diligently my speech, and my declaration with your ears.

chapters.zh: 我已陈明我的案，知道自己有义。
chapters.en: Behold now, I have ordered cause; I know that I shall be justified.

chapters.zh: 有谁与我争论，我就情愿缄默不言，气绝而亡。
chapters.en: Who he will plead with me? for now, if I hold my tongue, I shall give up the ghost.

chapters.zh: 惟有两件不要向我施行，我就不躲开你的面：
chapters.en: Only do not two unto me: then will I not hide myself from thee.

chapters.zh: 就是把你的手缩回，远离我身；又不使你的惊惶威吓我。
chapters.en: Withdraw thine hand far from me: and let not thy dread make me afraid.

chapters.zh: 这样，你呼叫，我就回答；或是让我说话，你回答我。
chapters.en: Then call thou, and I will answer: or let me speak, and answer thou me.

chapters.zh: 我的罪孽和罪过有多少呢？求你叫我知道我的过犯与罪愆。
chapters.en: How many mine iniquities and sins? make me to know my transgression and my sin.

chapters.zh: 你为何掩面、拿我当仇敌呢？
chapters.en: Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and holdest me for thine enemy?

chapters.zh: 你要惊动被风吹的叶子么？要追赶枯干的碎秸么？
chapters.en: Wilt thou break a leaf driven to and fro? and wilt thou pursue the dry stubble?

chapters.zh: 你按罪状刑罚我，又使我担当幼年的罪孽；
chapters.en: For thou writest bitter things against me, and makest me to possess the iniquities of my youth.

chapters.zh: 也把我的脚上了木狗，并窥察我一切的道路，为我的脚掌划定界限。
chapters.en: Thou puttest my feet also in the stocks, and lookest narrowly unto all my paths; thou settest a print upon the heels of my feet.

chapters.zh: 我已经像灭绝的烂物，像虫蛀的衣裳。
chapters.en: And he, as a rotten thing, consumeth, as a garment that is moth eaten.

chapters.zh: 人为妇人所生，日子短少，多有患难；
chapters.en: Man born of a woman of few days, and full of trouble.

chapters.zh: 出来如花，又被割下，飞去如影，不能存留。
chapters.en: He cometh forth like a flower, and is cut down: he fleeth also as a shadow, and continueth not.

chapters.zh: 这样的人你岂睁眼看他么？又叫我来受审么？
chapters.en: And dost thou open thine eyes upon such an one, and bringest me into judgment with thee?

chapters.zh: 谁能使洁净之物出于污秽之中呢？无论谁也不能！
chapters.en: Who can bring a clean out of an unclean? not one.

chapters.zh: 人的日子既然限定，他的月数在你那里，你也派定他的界限，使他不能越过，
chapters.en: Seeing his days determined, the number of his months with thee, thou hast appointed his bounds that he cannot pass;

chapters.zh: 便求你转眼不看他，使他得歇息，直等他像雇工人完毕他的日子。
chapters.en: Turn from him, that he may rest, till he shall accomplish, as an hireling, his day.

chapters.zh: 树若被砍下，还可指望发芽，嫩枝生长不息；
chapters.en: For there is hope of a tree, if it be cut down, that it will sprout again, and that the tender branch thereof will not cease.

chapters.zh: 其根虽然衰老在地里，干也死在土中，
chapters.en: Though the root thereof wax old in the earth, and the stock thereof die in the ground;

chapters.zh: 及至得了水气，还要发芽，又长枝条，像新栽的树一样。
chapters.en: through the scent of water it will bud, and bring forth boughs like a plant.

chapters.zh: 但人死亡而消灭；他气绝，竟在何处呢？
chapters.en: But man dieth, and wasteth away: yea, man giveth up the ghost, and where he?

chapters.zh: 海中的水绝尽，江河消散干涸。
chapters.en: the waters fail from the sea, and the flood decayeth and drieth up:

chapters.zh: 人也是如此，躺下不再起来，等到天没有了，仍不得复醒，也不得从睡中唤醒。
chapters.en: So man lieth down, and riseth not: till the heavens no more, they shall not awake, nor be raised out of their sleep.

chapters.zh: 惟愿你把我藏在阴间，存于隐密处，等你的忿怒过去；愿你为我定了日期，记念我。
chapters.en: O that thou wouldest hide me in the grave, that thou wouldest keep me secret, until thy wrath be past, that thou wouldest appoint me a set time, and remember me!

chapters.zh: 人若死了岂能再活呢？我只要在我一切争战的日子，等我被释放（或译：改变）的时候来到。
chapters.en: If a man die, shall he live ? all the days of my appointed time will I wait, till my change come.

chapters.zh: 你呼叫，我便回答；你手所做的，你必羡慕。
chapters.en: Thou shalt call, and I will answer thee: thou wilt have a desire to the work of thine hands.

chapters.zh: 但如今你数点我的脚步，岂不窥察我的罪过么？
chapters.en: For now thou numberest my steps: dost thou not watch over my sin?

chapters.zh: 我的过犯被你封在囊中，也缝严了我的罪孽。
chapters.en: My transgression sealed up in a bag, and thou sewest up mine iniquity.

chapters.zh: 山崩变为无有；磐石挪开原处。
chapters.en: And surely the mountain falling cometh to nought, and the rock is removed out of his place.

chapters.zh: 水流消磨石头，所流溢的洗去地上的尘土；你也照样灭绝人的指望。
chapters.en: The waters wear the stones: thou washest away the things which grow of the dust of the earth; and thou destroyest the hope of man.

chapters.zh: 你攻击人常常得胜，使他去世；你改变他的容貌，叫他往而不回。
chapters.en: Thou prevailest for ever against him, and he passeth: thou changest his countenance, and sendest him away.

chapters.zh: 他儿子得尊荣，他也不知道，降为卑，他也不觉得。
chapters.en: His sons come to honour, and he knoweth not; and they are brought low, but he perceiveth not of them.

chapters.zh: 但知身上疼痛，心中悲哀。
chapters.en: But his flesh upon him shall have pain, and his soul within him shall mourn.

chapters.zh: 提幔人以利法回答说：
chapters.en: Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite, and said,

chapters.zh: 智慧人岂可用虚空的知识回答，用东风充满肚腹呢？
chapters.en: Should a wise man utter vain knowledge, and fill his belly with the east wind?

chapters.zh: 他岂可用无益的话和无济于事的言语理论呢？
chapters.en: Should he reason with unprofitable talk? or with speeches wherewith he can do no good?

chapters.zh: 你是废弃敬畏的意，在神面前阻止敬虔的心。
chapters.en: Yea, thou castest off fear, and restrainest prayer before God.

chapters.zh: 你的罪孽指教你的口；你选用诡诈人的舌头。
chapters.en: For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity, and thou choosest the tongue of the crafty.

chapters.zh: 你自己的口定你有罪，并非是我；你自己的嘴见证你的不是。
chapters.en: Thine own mouth condemneth thee, and not I: yea, thine own lips testify against thee.

chapters.zh: 你岂是头一个被生的人么？你受造在诸山之先么？
chapters.en: thou the first man was born? or wast thou made before the hills?

chapters.zh: 你曾听见神的密旨么？你还将智慧独自得尽么？
chapters.en: Hast thou heard the secret of God? and dost thou restrain wisdom to thyself?

chapters.zh: 你知道甚么是我们不知道的呢？你明白甚么是我们不明白的呢？
chapters.en: What knowest thou, that we know not? understandest thou, which not in us?

chapters.zh: 我们这里有白发的和年纪老迈的，比你父亲还老。
chapters.en: With us both the grayheaded and very aged men, much elder than thy father.

chapters.zh: 神用温和的话安慰你，你以为太小么？
chapters.en: the consolations of God small with thee? is there any secret thing with thee?

chapters.zh: 你的心为何将你逼去？你的眼为何冒出火星，
chapters.en: Why doth thine heart carry thee away? and what do thy eyes wink at,

chapters.zh: 使你的灵反对神，也任你的口发这言语？
chapters.en: That thou turnest thy spirit against God, and lettest words go out of thy mouth?

chapters.zh: 人是甚么，竟算为洁净呢？妇人所生的是甚么，竟算为义呢？
chapters.en: What man, that he should be clean? and born of a woman, that he should be righteous?

chapters.zh: 神不信靠他的众圣者；在他眼前，天也不洁净，
chapters.en: Behold, he putteth no trust in his saints; yea, the heavens are not clean in his sight.

chapters.zh: 何况那污秽可憎、喝罪孽如水的世人呢！
chapters.en: How much more abominable and filthy man, which drinketh iniquity like water?

chapters.zh: 我指示你，你要听；我要述说所看见的，
chapters.en: I will shew thee, hear me; and that I have seen I will declare;

chapters.zh: 就是智慧人从列祖所受，传说而不隐瞒的。
chapters.en: Which wise men have told from their fathers, and have not hid :

chapters.zh: （这地惟独赐给他们，并没有外人从他们中间经过。）
chapters.en: Unto whom alone the earth was given, and no stranger passed among them.

chapters.zh: 恶人一生之日劬劳痛苦；强暴人一生的年数也是如此。
chapters.en: The wicked man travaileth with pain all days, and the number of years is hidden to the oppressor.

chapters.zh: 惊吓的声音常在他耳中；在平安时，抢夺的必临到他那里。
chapters.en: A dreadful sound in his ears: in prosperity the destroyer shall come upon him.

chapters.zh: 他不信自己能从黑暗中转回；他被刀剑等候。
chapters.en: He believeth not that he shall return out of darkness, and he is waited for of the sword.

chapters.zh: 他漂流在外求食，说：哪里有食物呢？他知道黑暗的日子在他手边预备好了。
chapters.en: He wandereth abroad for bread, , Where ? he knoweth that the day of darkness is ready at his hand.

chapters.zh: 急难困苦叫他害怕，而且胜了他，好像君王预备上阵一样。
chapters.en: Trouble and anguish shall make him afraid; they shall prevail against him, as a king ready to the battle.

chapters.zh: 他伸手攻击神，以骄傲攻击全能者，
chapters.en: For he stretcheth out his hand against God, and strengtheneth himself against the Almighty.

chapters.zh: 挺著颈项，用盾牌的厚凸面向全能者直闯；
chapters.en: He runneth upon him, on neck, upon the thick bosses of his bucklers:

chapters.zh: 是因他的脸蒙上脂油，腰积成肥肉。
chapters.en: Because he covereth his face with his fatness, and maketh collops of fat on flanks.

chapters.zh: 他曾住在荒凉城邑，无人居住、将成乱堆的房屋。
chapters.en: And he dwelleth in desolate cities, in houses which no man inhabiteth, which are ready to become heaps.

chapters.zh: 他不得富足，财物不得常存，产业在地上也不加增。
chapters.en: He shall not be rich, neither shall his substance continue, neither shall he prolong the perfection thereof upon the earth.

chapters.zh: 他不得出离黑暗。火燄要将他的枝子烧干；因神口中的气，他要灭亡（原文是走去）。
chapters.en: He shall not depart out of darkness; the flame shall dry up his branches, and by the breath of his mouth shall he go away.

chapters.zh: 他不用倚靠虚假欺哄自己，因虚假必成为他的报应。
chapters.en: Let not him that is deceived trust in vanity: for vanity shall be his recompence.

chapters.zh: 他的日期未到之先，这事必成就；他的枝子不得青绿。
chapters.en: It shall be accomplished before his time, and his branch shall not be green.

chapters.zh: 他必像葡萄树的葡萄，未熟而落；又像橄榄树的花，一开而谢。
chapters.en: He shall shake off his unripe grape as the vine, and shall cast off his flower as the olive.

chapters.zh: 原来不敬虔之辈必无生育；受贿赂之人的帐棚必被火烧。
chapters.en: For the congregation of hypocrites desolate, and fire shall consume the tabernacles of bribery.

chapters.zh: 他们所怀的是毒害，所生的是罪孽；心里所预备的是诡诈。
chapters.en: They conceive mischief, and bring forth vanity, and their belly prepareth deceit.

chapters.zh: 约伯回答说：
chapters.en: Then Job answered and said,

chapters.zh: 这样的话我听了许多；你们安慰人，反叫人愁烦。
chapters.en: I have heard many such things: miserable comforters ye all.

chapters.zh: 虚空的言语有穷尽么？有甚么话惹动你回答呢？
chapters.en: Shall vain words have an end? or what emboldeneth thee that thou answerest?

chapters.zh: 我也能说你们那样的话；你们若处在我的境遇，我也会联络言语攻击你们，又能向你们摇头。
chapters.en: I also could speak as ye : if your soul were in my soul's stead, I could heap up words against you, and shake mine head at you.

chapters.zh: 但我必用口坚固你们，用嘴消解你们的忧愁。
chapters.en: I would strengthen you with my mouth, and the moving of my lips should asswage .

chapters.zh: 我虽说话，忧愁仍不得消解；我虽停住不说，忧愁就离开我么？
chapters.en: Though I speak, my grief is not asswaged: and I forbear, what am I eased?

chapters.zh: 但现在神使我困倦，使亲友远离我，
chapters.en: But now he hath made me weary: thou hast made desolate all my company.

chapters.zh: 又抓住我，作见证攻击我；我身体的枯瘦也当面见证我的不是。
chapters.en: And thou hast filled me with wrinkles, is a witness : and my leanness rising up in me beareth witness to my face.

chapters.zh: 主发怒撕裂我，逼迫我，向我切齿；我的敌人怒目看我。
chapters.en: He teareth in his wrath, who hateth me: he gnasheth upon me with his teeth; mine enemy sharpeneth his eyes upon me.

chapters.zh: 他们向我开口，打我的脸羞辱我，聚会攻击我。
chapters.en: They have gaped upon me with their mouth; they have smitten me upon the cheek reproachfully; they have gathered themselves together against me.

chapters.zh: 神把我交给不敬虔的人，把我扔到恶人的手中。
chapters.en: God hath delivered me to the ungodly, and turned me over into the hands of the wicked.

chapters.zh: 我素来安逸，他折断我，掐住我的颈项，把我摔碎，又立我为他的箭靶子。
chapters.en: I was at ease, but he hath broken me asunder: he hath also taken by my neck, and shaken me to pieces, and set me up for his mark.

chapters.zh: 他的弓箭手四面围绕我；他破裂我的肺腑，并不留情，把我的胆倾倒在地上，
chapters.en: His archers compass me round about, he cleaveth my reins asunder, and doth not spare; he poureth out my gall upon the ground.

chapters.zh: 将我破裂又破裂，如同勇士向我直闯。
chapters.en: He breaketh me with breach upon breach, he runneth upon me like a giant.

chapters.zh: 我缝麻布在我皮肤上，把我的角放在尘土中。
chapters.en: I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin, and defiled my horn in the dust.

chapters.zh: 我的脸因哭泣发紫，在我的眼皮上有死荫。
chapters.en: My face is foul with weeping, and on my eyelids the shadow of death;

chapters.zh: 我的手中却无强暴；我的祈祷也是清洁。
chapters.en: Not for injustice in mine hands: also my prayer pure.

chapters.zh: 地啊，不要遮盖我的血！不要阻挡我的哀求！
chapters.en: O earth, cover not thou my blood, and let my cry have no place.

chapters.zh: 现今，在天有我的见证，在上有我的中保。
chapters.en: Also now, behold, my witness in heaven, and my record on high.

chapters.zh: 我的朋友讥诮我，我却向神眼泪汪汪。
chapters.en: My friends scorn me: mine eye poureth out unto God.

chapters.zh: 愿人得与神辩白，如同人与朋友辩白一样；
chapters.en: O that one might plead for a man with God, as a man for his neighbour!

chapters.zh: 因为再过几年，我必走那往而不返之路。
chapters.en: When a few years are come, then I shall go the way I shall not return.

chapters.zh: 我的心灵消耗，我的日子灭尽；坟墓为我预备好了。
chapters.en: My breath is corrupt, my days are extinct, the graves for me.

chapters.zh: 真有戏笑我的在我这里，我眼常见他们惹动我。
chapters.en: not mockers with me? and doth not mine eye continue in their provocation?

chapters.zh: 愿主拿凭据给我，自己为我作保。在你以外谁肯与我击掌呢？
chapters.en: Lay down now, put me in a surety with thee; who he will strike hands with me?

chapters.zh: 因你使他们心不明理，所以你必不高举他们。
chapters.en: For thou hast hid their heart from understanding: therefore shalt thou not exalt .

chapters.zh: 控告他的朋友、以朋友为可抢夺的，连他儿女的眼睛也要失明。
chapters.en: He that speaketh flattery to friends, even the eyes of his children shall fail.

chapters.zh: 神使我作了民中的笑谈；他们也吐唾沫在我脸上。
chapters.en: He hath made me also a byword of the people; and aforetime I was as a tabret.

chapters.zh: 我的眼睛因忧愁昏花；我的百体好像影儿。
chapters.en: Mine eye also is dim by reason of sorrow, and all my members as a shadow.

chapters.zh: 正直人因此必惊奇；无辜的人要兴起攻击不敬虔之辈。
chapters.en: Upright shall be astonied at this, and the innocent shall stir up himself against the hypocrite.

chapters.zh: 然而，义人要持守所行的道；手洁的人要力上加力。
chapters.en: The righteous also shall hold on his way, and he that hath clean hands shall be stronger and stronger.

chapters.zh: 至于你们众人，可以再来辩论罢！你们中间，我找不著一个智慧人。
chapters.en: But as for you all, do ye return, and come now: for I cannot find wise among you.

chapters.zh: 我的日子已经过了；我的谋算、我心所想望的已经断绝。
chapters.en: My days are past, my purposes are broken off, the thoughts of my heart.

chapters.zh: 他们以黑夜为白昼，说：亮光近乎黑暗。
chapters.en: They change the night into day: the light short because of darkness.

chapters.zh: 我若盼望阴间为我的房屋，若下榻在黑暗中，
chapters.en: If I wait, the grave mine house: I have made my bed in the darkness.

chapters.zh: 若对朽坏说：你是我的父；对虫说：你是我的母亲姊妹；
chapters.en: I have said to corruption, Thou my father: to the worm, my mother, and my sister.

chapters.zh: 这样，我的指望在哪里呢？我所指望的谁能看见呢？
chapters.en: And where now my hope? as for my hope, who shall see it?

chapters.zh: 等到安息在尘土中，这指望必下到阴间的门闩那里了。
chapters.en: They shall go down to the bars of the pit, when rest together in the dust.

chapters.zh: 书亚人比勒达回答说：
chapters.en: Then answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,

chapters.zh: 你寻索言语要到几时呢？你可以揣摩思想，然后我们就说话。
chapters.en: How long ye make an end of words? mark, and afterwards we will speak.

chapters.zh: 我们为何算为畜生，在你眼中看作污秽呢？
chapters.en: Wherefore are we counted as beasts, reputed vile in your sight?

chapters.zh: 你这恼怒将自己撕裂的，难道大地为你见弃、磐石挪开原处么？
chapters.en: He teareth himself in his anger: shall the earth be forsaken for thee? and shall the rock be removed out of his place?

chapters.zh: 恶人的亮光必要熄灭；他的火燄必不照耀。
chapters.en: Yea, the light of the wicked shall be put out, and the spark of his fire shall not shine.

chapters.zh: 他帐棚中的亮光要变为黑暗；他以上的灯也必熄灭。
chapters.en: The light shall be dark in his tabernacle, and his candle shall be put out with him.

chapters.zh: 他坚强的脚步必见狭窄；自己的计谋必将他绊倒。
chapters.en: The steps of his strength shall be straitened, and his own counsel shall cast him down.

chapters.zh: 因为他被自己的脚陷入网中，走在缠人的网罗上。
chapters.en: For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he walketh upon a snare.

chapters.zh: 圈套必抓住他的脚跟；机关必擒获他。
chapters.en: The gin shall take by the heel, the robber shall prevail against him.

chapters.zh: 活扣为他藏在土内；羁绊为他藏在路上。
chapters.en: The snare laid for him in the ground, and a trap for him in the way.

chapters.zh: 四面的惊吓要使他害怕，并且追赶他的脚跟。
chapters.en: Terrors shall make him afraid on every side, and shall drive him to his feet.

chapters.zh: 他的力量必因饥饿衰败；祸患要在他旁边等候。
chapters.en: His strength shall be hungerbitten, and destruction ready at his side.

chapters.zh: 他本身的肢体要被吞吃；死亡的长子要吞吃他的肢体。
chapters.en: It shall devour the strength of his skin: the firstborn of death shall devour his strength.

chapters.zh: 他要从所倚靠的帐棚被拔出来，带到惊吓的王那里。
chapters.en: His confidence shall be rooted out of his tabernacle, and it shall bring him to the king of terrors.

chapters.zh: 不属他的必住在他的帐棚里；硫磺必撒在他所住之处。
chapters.en: It shall dwell in his tabernacle, because none of his: brimstone shall be scattered upon his habitation.

chapters.zh: 下边，他的根本要枯干；上边，他的枝子要剪除。
chapters.en: His roots shall be dried up beneath, and above shall his branch be cut off.

chapters.zh: 他的记念在地上必然灭亡；他的名字在街上也不存留。
chapters.en: His remembrance shall perish from the earth, and he shall have no name in the street.

chapters.zh: 他必从光明中被撵到黑暗里，必被赶出世界。
chapters.en: He shall be driven from light into darkness, and chased out of the world.

chapters.zh: 在本民中必无子无孙；在寄居之地也无一人存留。
chapters.en: He shall neither have son nor nephew among his people, nor any remaining in his dwellings.

chapters.zh: 以后来的要惊奇他的日子，好像以前去的受了惊骇。
chapters.en: They that come after shall be astonied at his day, as they that went before were affrighted.

chapters.zh: 不义之人的住处总是这样；此乃不认识神之人的地步。
chapters.en: Surely such the dwellings of the wicked, and this the place knoweth not God.

chapters.zh: 约伯回答说：
chapters.en: Then Job answered and said,

chapters.zh: 你们搅扰我的心，用言语压碎我要到几时呢？
chapters.en: How long will ye vex my soul, and break me in pieces with words?

chapters.zh: 你们这十次羞辱我；你们苦待我也不以为耻。
chapters.en: These ten times have ye reproached me: ye are not ashamed ye make yourselves strange to me.

chapters.zh: 果真我有错，这错乃是在我。
chapters.en: And be it indeed I have erred, mine error remaineth with myself.

chapters.zh: 你们果然要向我夸大，以我的羞辱为证指责我，
chapters.en: If indeed ye will magnify against me, and plead against me my reproach:

chapters.zh: 就该知道是神倾覆我，用网罗围绕我。
chapters.en: Know now that God hath overthrown me, and hath compassed me with his net.

chapters.zh: 我因委曲呼叫，却不蒙应允；我呼求，却不得公断。
chapters.en: Behold, I cry out of wrong, but I am not heard: I cry aloud, but no judgment.

chapters.zh: 神用篱笆拦住我的道路，使我不得经过；又使我的路径黑暗。
chapters.en: He hath fenced up my way that I cannot pass, and he hath set darkness in my paths.

chapters.zh: 他剥去我的荣光，摘去我头上的冠冕。
chapters.en: He hath stripped me of my glory, and taken the crown my head.

chapters.zh: 他在四围攻击我，我便归于死亡，将我的指望如树拔出来。
chapters.en: He hath destroyed me on every side, and I am gone: and mine hope hath he removed like a tree.

chapters.zh: 他的忿怒向我发作，以我为敌人。
chapters.en: He hath also kindled his wrath against me, and he counteth me unto him as his enemies.

chapters.zh: 他的军旅一齐上来，修筑战路攻击我，在我帐棚的四围安营。
chapters.en: His troops come together, and raise up their way against me, and encamp round about my tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 他把我的弟兄隔在远处，使我所认识的全然与我生疏。
chapters.en: He hath put my brethren far from me, and mine acquaintance are verily estranged from me.

chapters.zh: 我的亲戚与我断绝；我的密友都忘记我。
chapters.en: My kinsfolk have failed, and my familiar friends have forgotten me.

chapters.zh: 在我家寄居的，和我的使女都以我为外人；我在他们眼中看为外邦人。
chapters.en: They that dwell in mine house, and my maids, count me for a stranger: I am an alien in their sight.

chapters.zh: 我呼唤仆人，虽用口求他，他还是不回答。
chapters.en: I called my servant, and he gave no answer; I intreated him with my mouth.

chapters.zh: 我口的气味，我妻子厌恶；我的恳求，我同胞也憎嫌。
chapters.en: My breath is strange to my wife, though I intreated for the children's of mine own body.

chapters.zh: 连小孩子也藐视我；我若起来，他们都嘲笑我。
chapters.en: Yea, young children despised me; I arose, and they spake against me.

chapters.zh: 我的密友都憎恶我；我平日所爱的人向我翻脸。
chapters.en: All my inward friends abhorred me: and they whom I loved are turned against me.

chapters.zh: 我的皮肉紧贴骨头；我只剩牙皮逃脱了。
chapters.en: My bone cleaveth to my skin and to my flesh, and I am escaped with the skin of my teeth.

chapters.zh: 我朋友啊，可怜我！可怜我！因为神的手攻击我。
chapters.en: Have pity upon me, have pity upon me, O ye my friends; for the hand of God hath touched me.

chapters.zh: 你们为甚么彷佛神逼迫我，吃我的肉还以为不足呢？
chapters.en: Why do ye persecute me as God, and are not satisfied with my flesh?

chapters.zh: 惟愿我的言语现在写上，都记录在书上；
chapters.en: Oh that my words were now written! oh that they were printed in a book!

chapters.zh: 用铁笔镌刻，用铅灌在磐石上，直存到永远。
chapters.en: That they were graven with an iron pen and lead in the rock for ever!

chapters.zh: 我知道我的救赎主活著，末了必站立在地上。
chapters.en: For I know my redeemer liveth, and he shall stand at the latter upon the earth:

chapters.zh: 我这皮肉灭绝之后，我必在肉体之外得见神。
chapters.en: And after my skin destroy this , yet in my flesh shall I see God:

chapters.zh: 我自己要见他，亲眼要看他，并不像外人。我的心肠在我里面消灭了！
chapters.en: Whom I shall see for myself, and mine eyes shall behold, and not another; my reins be consumed within me.

chapters.zh: 你们若说：我们逼迫他要何等地重呢？惹事的根乃在乎他；
chapters.en: But ye should say, Why persecute we him, seeing the root of the matter is found in me?

chapters.zh: 你们就当惧怕刀剑；因为忿怒惹动刀剑的刑罚，使你们知道有报应（原文是审判）。
chapters.en: Be ye afraid of the sword: for wrath the punishments of the sword, that ye may know a judgment.

chapters.zh: 拿玛人琐法回答说：
chapters.en: Then answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said,

chapters.zh: 我心中急躁，所以我的思念叫我回答。
chapters.en: Therefore do my thoughts cause me to answer, and for I make haste.

chapters.zh: 我已听见那羞辱我，责备我的话；我的悟性叫我回答。
chapters.en: I have heard the check of my reproach, and the spirit of my understanding causeth me to answer.

chapters.zh: 你岂不知亘古以来，自从人生在地，
chapters.en: Knowest thou this of old, since man was placed upon earth,

chapters.zh: 恶人夸胜是暂时的，不敬虔人的喜乐不过转眼之间么？
chapters.en: That the triumphing of the wicked short, and the joy of the hypocrite for a moment?

chapters.zh: 他的尊荣虽达到天上，头虽顶到云中，
chapters.en: Though his excellency mount up to the heavens, and his head reach unto the clouds;

chapters.zh: 他终必灭亡，像自己的粪一样；素来见他的人要说：他在哪里呢？
chapters.en: he shall perish for ever like his own dung: they which have seen him shall say, Where he?

chapters.zh: 他必飞去如梦，不再寻见，速被赶去，如夜间的异象。
chapters.en: He shall fly away as a dream, and shall not be found: yea, he shall be chased away as a vision of the night.

chapters.zh: 亲眼见过他的，必不再见他；他的本处也再见不著他。
chapters.en: The eye also saw him shall no more; neither shall his place any more behold him.

chapters.zh: 他的儿女要求穷人的恩；他的手要赔还不义之财。
chapters.en: His children shall seek to please the poor, and his hands shall restore their goods.

chapters.zh: 他的骨头虽然有青年之力，却要和他一同躺卧在尘土中。
chapters.en: His bones are full of his youth, which shall lie down with him in the dust.

chapters.zh: 他口内虽以恶为甘甜，藏在舌头底下，
chapters.en: Though wickedness be sweet in his mouth, he hide it under his tongue;

chapters.zh: 爱恋不舍，含在口中；
chapters.en: he spare it, and forsake it not; but keep it still within his mouth:

chapters.zh: 他的食物在肚里却要化为酸，在他里面成为虺蛇的恶毒。
chapters.en: his meat in his bowels is turned, the gall of asps within him.

chapters.zh: 他吞了财宝，还要吐出；神要从他腹中掏出来。
chapters.en: He hath swallowed down riches, and he shall vomit them up again: God shall cast them out of his belly.

chapters.zh: 他必吸饮虺蛇的毒气；蝮蛇的舌头也必杀他。
chapters.en: He shall suck the poison of asps: the viper's tongue shall slay him.

chapters.zh: 流奶与蜜之河，他不得再见。
chapters.en: He shall not see the rivers, the floods, the brooks of honey and butter.

chapters.zh: 他劳碌得来的要赔还，不得享用（原文是吞下）；不能照所得的财货欢乐。
chapters.en: That which he laboured for shall he restore, and shall not swallow down: according to substance the restitution , and he shall not rejoice .

chapters.zh: 他欺压穷人，且又离弃；强取非自己所盖的房屋（或译：强取房屋不得再建造）。
chapters.en: Because he hath oppressed hath forsaken the poor; he hath violently taken away an house which he builded not;

chapters.zh: 他因贪而无厌，所喜悦的连一样也不能保守。
chapters.en: Surely he shall not feel quietness in his belly, he shall not save of that which he desired.

chapters.zh: 其余的没有一样他不吞灭，所以他的福乐不能长久。
chapters.en: There shall none of his meat be left; therefore shall no man look for his goods.

chapters.zh: 他在满足有余的时候，必到狭窄的地步；凡受苦楚的人都必加手在他身上。
chapters.en: In the fulness of his sufficiency he shall be in straits: every hand of the wicked shall come upon him.

chapters.zh: 他正要充满肚腹的时候，神必将猛烈的忿怒降在他身上；正在他吃饭的时候，要将这忿怒像雨降在他身上。
chapters.en: he is about to fill his belly, shall cast the fury of his wrath upon him, and shall rain upon him while he is eating.

chapters.zh: 他要躲避铁器；铜弓的箭要将他射透。
chapters.en: He shall flee from the iron weapon, the bow of steel shall strike him through.

chapters.zh: 他把箭一抽，就从他身上出来；发光的箭头从他胆中出来，有惊惶临在他身上。
chapters.en: It is drawn, and cometh out of the body; yea, the glittering sword cometh out of his gall: terrors upon him.

chapters.zh: 他的财宝归于黑暗；人所不吹的火要把他烧灭，要把他帐棚中所剩下的烧毁。
chapters.en: All darkness hid in his secret places: a fire not blown shall consume him; it shall go ill with him that is left in his tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 天要显明他的罪孽；地要兴起攻击他。
chapters.en: The heaven shall reveal his iniquity; and the earth shall rise up against him.

chapters.zh: 他的家产必然过去；神发怒的日子，他的货物都要消灭。
chapters.en: The increase of his house shall depart, shall flow away in the day of his wrath.

chapters.zh: 这是恶人从神所得的分，是神为他所定的产业。
chapters.en: This the portion of a wicked man from God, and the heritage appointed unto him by God.

chapters.zh: 约伯回答说：
chapters.en: But Job answered and said,

chapters.zh: 你们要细听我的言语，就算是你们安慰我。
chapters.en: Hear diligently my speech, and let this be your consolations.

chapters.zh: 请宽容我，我又要说话；说了以后，任凭你们嗤笑罢！
chapters.en: Suffer me that I may speak; and after that I have spoken, mock on.

chapters.zh: 我岂是向人诉冤？为何不焦急呢？
chapters.en: As for me, my complaint to man? and if , why should not my spirit be troubled?

chapters.zh: 你们要看著我而惊奇，用手摀口。
chapters.en: Mark me, and be astonished, and lay hand upon mouth.

chapters.zh: 我每逢思想，心就惊惶，浑身战兢。
chapters.en: Even when I remember I am afraid, and trembling taketh hold on my flesh.

chapters.zh: 恶人为何存活，享大寿数，势力强盛呢？
chapters.en: Wherefore do the wicked live, become old, yea, are mighty in power?

chapters.zh: 他们眼见儿孙，和他们一同坚立。
chapters.en: Their seed is established in their sight with them, and their offspring before their eyes.

chapters.zh: 他们的家宅平安无惧；神的杖也不加在他们身上。
chapters.en: Their houses safe from fear, neither the rod of God upon them.

chapters.zh: 他们的公牛孳生而不断绝；母牛下犊而不掉胎。
chapters.en: Their bull gendereth, and faileth not; their cow calveth, and casteth not her calf.

chapters.zh: 他们打发小孩子出去，多如羊群；他们的儿女踊跃跳舞。
chapters.en: They send forth their little ones like a flock, and their children dance.

chapters.zh: 他们随著琴鼓歌唱，又因箫声欢喜。
chapters.en: They take the timbrel and harp, and rejoice at the sound of the organ.

chapters.zh: 他们度日诸事亨通，转眼下入阴间。
chapters.en: They spend their days in wealth, and in a moment go down to the grave.

chapters.zh: 他们对神说：离开我们罢！我们不愿晓得你的道。
chapters.en: Therefore they say unto God, Depart from us; for we desire not the knowledge of thy ways.

chapters.zh: 全能者是谁，我们何必事奉他呢？求告他有甚么益处呢？
chapters.en: What the Almighty, that we should serve him? and what profit should we have, if we pray unto him?

chapters.zh: 看哪，他们亨通不在乎自己；恶人所谋定的离我好远。
chapters.en: Lo, their good not in their hand: the counsel of the wicked is far from me.

chapters.zh: 恶人的灯何尝熄灭？患难何尝临到他们呢？神何尝发怒，向他们分散灾祸呢？
chapters.en: How oft is the candle of the wicked put out! and cometh their destruction upon them! distributeth sorrows in his anger.

chapters.zh: 他们何尝像风前的碎秸，如暴风刮去的糠秕呢？
chapters.en: They are as stubble before the wind, and as chaff that the storm carrieth away.

chapters.zh: 你们说：神为恶人的儿女积蓄罪孽；我说：不如本人受报，好使他亲自知道。
chapters.en: God layeth up his iniquity for his children: he rewardeth him, and he shall know .

chapters.zh: 愿他亲眼看见自己败亡，亲自饮全能者的忿怒。
chapters.en: His eyes shall see his destruction, and he shall drink of the wrath of the Almighty.

chapters.zh: 他的岁月既尽，他还顾他本家么？
chapters.en: For what pleasure he in his house after him, when the number of his months is cut off in the midst?

chapters.zh: 神既审判那在高位的，谁能将知识教训他呢？
chapters.en: Shall teach God knowledge? seeing he judgeth those that are high.

chapters.zh: 有人至死身体强壮，尽得平靖安逸；
chapters.en: One dieth in his full strength, being wholly at ease and quiet.

chapters.zh: 他的奶桶充满，他的骨髓滋润。
chapters.en: His breasts are full of milk, and his bones are moistened with marrow.

chapters.zh: 有人至死心中痛苦，终身未尝福乐的滋味；
chapters.en: And another dieth in the bitterness of his soul, and never eateth with pleasure.

chapters.zh: 他们一样躺卧在尘土中，都被虫子遮盖。
chapters.en: They shall lie down alike in the dust, and the worms shall cover them.

chapters.zh: 我知道你们的意思，并诬害我的计谋。
chapters.en: Behold, I know your thoughts, and the devices ye wrongfully imagine against me.

chapters.zh: 你们说：霸者的房屋在哪里？恶人住过的帐棚在哪里？
chapters.en: For ye say, Where the house of the prince? and where the dwelling places of the wicked?

chapters.zh: 你们岂没有询问过路的人么？不知道他们所引的证据么？
chapters.en: Have ye not asked them that go by the way? and do ye not know their tokens,

chapters.zh: 就是恶人在祸患的日子得存留，在发怒的日子得逃脱。
chapters.en: That the wicked is reserved to the day of destruction? they shall be brought forth to the day of wrath.

chapters.zh: 他所行的，有谁当面给他说明；他所做的，有谁报应他呢？
chapters.en: Who shall declare his way to his face? and who shall repay him he hath done?

chapters.zh: 然而他要被抬到茔地；并有人看守坟墓。
chapters.en: Yet shall he be brought to the grave, and shall remain in the tomb.

chapters.zh: 他要以谷中的土块为甘甜；在他以先去的无数，在他以后去的更多。
chapters.en: The clods of the valley shall be sweet unto him, and every man shall draw after him, as innumerable before him.

chapters.zh: 你们对答的话中既都错谬，怎么徒然安慰我呢？
chapters.en: How then comfort ye me in vain, seeing in your answers there remaineth falsehood?

chapters.zh: 提幔人以利法回答说：
chapters.en: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,

chapters.zh: 人岂能使神有益呢？智慧人但能有益于己。
chapters.en: Can a man be profitable unto God, as he that is wise may be profitable unto himself?

chapters.zh: 你为人公义，岂叫全能者喜悦呢？你行为完全，岂能使他得利呢？
chapters.en: any pleasure to the Almighty, that thou art righteous? or gain , that thou makest thy ways perfect?

chapters.zh: 岂是因你敬畏他就责备你、审判你么？
chapters.en: Will he reprove thee for fear of thee? will he enter with thee into judgment?

chapters.zh: 你的罪恶岂不是大么？你的罪孽也没有穷尽；
chapters.en: not thy wickedness great? and thine iniquities infinite?

chapters.zh: 因你无故强取弟兄的物为当头，剥去贫寒人的衣服。
chapters.en: For thou hast taken a pledge from thy brother for nought, and stripped the naked of their clothing.

chapters.zh: 困乏的人，你没有给他水喝；饥饿的人，你没有给他食物。
chapters.en: Thou hast not given water to the weary to drink, and thou hast withholden bread from the hungry.

chapters.zh: 有能力的人就得地土；尊贵的人也住在其中。
chapters.en: But the mighty man, he had the earth; and the honourable man dwelt in it.

chapters.zh: 你打发寡妇空手回去，折断孤儿的膀臂。
chapters.en: Thou hast sent widows away empty, and the arms of the fatherless have been broken.

chapters.zh: 因此，有网罗环绕你，有恐惧忽然使你惊惶；
chapters.en: Therefore snares round about thee, and sudden fear troubleth thee;

chapters.zh: 或有黑暗蒙蔽你，并有洪水淹没你。
chapters.en: Or darkness, thou canst not see; and abundance of waters cover thee.

chapters.zh: 神岂不是在高天么？你看星宿何其高呢！
chapters.en: not God in the height of heaven? and behold the height of the stars, how high they are!

chapters.zh: 你说：神知道甚么？他岂能看透幽暗施行审判呢？
chapters.en: And thou sayest, How doth God know? can he judge through the dark cloud?

chapters.zh: 密云将他遮盖，使他不能看见；他周游穹苍。
chapters.en: Thick clouds a covering to him, that he seeth not; and he walketh in the circuit of heaven.

chapters.zh: 你要依从上古的道么？这道是恶人所行的。
chapters.en: Hast thou marked the old way which wicked men have trodden?

chapters.zh: 他们未到死期，忽然除灭；根基毁坏，好像被江河冲去。
chapters.en: Which were cut down out of time, whose foundation was overflown with a flood:

chapters.zh: 他们向神说：离开我们罢！又说：全能者能把我们怎么样呢？
chapters.en: Which said unto God, Depart from us: and what can the Almighty do for them?

chapters.zh: 哪知神以美物充满他们的房屋；但恶人所谋定的离我好远。
chapters.en: Yet he filled their houses with good : but the counsel of the wicked is far from me.

chapters.zh: 义人看见他们的结局就欢喜；无辜的人嗤笑他们，
chapters.en: The righteous see , and are glad: and the innocent laugh them to scorn.

chapters.zh: 说：那起来攻击我们的果然被剪除，其余的都被火烧灭。
chapters.en: Whereas our substance is not cut down, but the remnant of them the fire consumeth.

chapters.zh: 你要认识神，就得平安；福气也必临到你。
chapters.en: Acquaint now thyself with him, and be at peace: thereby good shall come unto thee.

chapters.zh: 你当领受他口中的教训，将他的言语存在心里。
chapters.en: Receive, I pray thee, the law from his mouth, and lay up his words in thine heart.

chapters.zh: 你若归向全能者，从你帐棚中远除不义，就必得建立。
chapters.en: If thou return to the Almighty, thou shalt be built up, thou shalt put away iniquity far from thy tabernacles.

chapters.zh: 要将你的珍宝丢在尘土里，将俄斐的黄金丢在溪河石头之间；
chapters.en: Then shalt thou lay up gold as dust, and the of Ophir as the stones of the brooks.

chapters.zh: 全能者就必为你的珍宝，作你的宝银。
chapters.en: Yea, the Almighty shall be thy defence, and thou shalt have plenty of silver.

chapters.zh: 你就要以全能者为喜乐，向神仰起脸来。
chapters.en: For then shalt thou have thy delight in the Almighty, and shalt lift up thy face unto God.

chapters.zh: 你要祷告他，他就听你；你也要还你的愿。
chapters.en: Thou shalt make thy prayer unto him, and he shall hear thee, and thou shalt pay thy vows.

chapters.zh: 你定意要做何事，必然给你成就；亮光也必照耀你的路。
chapters.en: Thou shalt also decree a thing, and it shall be established unto thee: and the light shall shine upon thy ways.

chapters.zh: 人使你降卑，你仍可说：必得高升；谦卑的人，神必然拯救。
chapters.en: When are cast down, then thou shalt say, lifting up; and he shall save the humble person.

chapters.zh: 人非无辜，神且要搭救他；他因你手中清洁，必蒙拯救。
chapters.en: He shall deliver the island of the innocent: and it is delivered by the pureness of thine hands.

chapters.zh: 约伯回答说：
chapters.en: Then Job answered and said,

chapters.zh: 如今我的哀告还算为悖逆；我的责罚比我的唉哼还重。
chapters.en: Even to day my complaint bitter: my stroke is heavier than my groaning.

chapters.zh: 惟愿我能知道在哪里可以寻见神，能到他的台前，
chapters.en: Oh that I knew where I might find him! I might come to his seat!

chapters.zh: 我就在他面前将我的案件陈明，满口辩白。
chapters.en: I would order cause before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.

chapters.zh: 我必知道他回答我的言语，明白他向我所说的话。
chapters.en: I would know the words he would answer me, and understand what he would say unto me.

chapters.zh: 他岂用大能与我争辩么？必不这样！他必理会我。
chapters.en: Will he plead against me with great power? No; but he would put in me.

chapters.zh: 在他那里正直人可以与他辩论；这样，我必永远脱离那审判我的。
chapters.en: There the righteous might dispute with him; so should I be delivered for ever from my judge.

chapters.zh: 只是，我往前行，他不在那里，往后退，也不能见他。
chapters.en: Behold, I go forward, but he not ; and backward, but I cannot perceive him:

chapters.zh: 他在左边行事，我却不能看见，在右边隐藏，我也不能见他。
chapters.en: On the left hand, where he doth work, but I cannot behold : he hideth himself on the right hand, that I cannot see :

chapters.zh: 然而他知道我所行的路；他试炼我之后，我必如精金。
chapters.en: But he knoweth the way that I take: he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold.

chapters.zh: 我脚追随他的步履；我谨守他的道，并不偏离。
chapters.en: My foot hath held his steps, his way have I kept, and not declined.

chapters.zh: 他嘴唇的命令，我未曾背弃；我看重他口中的言语，过于我需用的饮食。
chapters.en: Neither have I gone back from the commandment of his lips; I have esteemed the words of his mouth more than my necessary .

chapters.zh: 只是他心志已定，谁能使他转意呢？他心里所愿的，就行出来。
chapters.en: But he in one , and who can turn him? and his soul desireth, even he doeth.

chapters.zh: 他向我所定的，就必做成；这类的事他还有许多。
chapters.en: For he performeth appointed for me: and many such with him.

chapters.zh: 所以我在他面前惊惶；我思念这事便惧怕他。
chapters.en: Therefore am I troubled at his presence: when I consider, I am afraid of him.

chapters.zh: 神使我丧胆；全能者使我惊惶。
chapters.en: For God maketh my heart soft, and the Almighty troubleth me:

chapters.zh: 我的恐惧不是因为黑暗，也不是因为幽暗蒙蔽我的脸。
chapters.en: Because I was not cut off before the darkness, hath he covered the darkness from my face.

chapters.zh: 全能者既定期罚恶，为何不使认识他的人看见那日子呢？
chapters.en: Why, seeing times are not hidden from the Almighty, do they that know him not see his days?

chapters.zh: 有人挪移地界，抢夺群畜而牧养。
chapters.en: remove the landmarks; they violently take away flocks, and feed .

chapters.zh: 他们拉去孤儿的驴，强取寡妇的牛为当头。
chapters.en: They drive away the ass of the fatherless, they take the widow's ox for a pledge.

chapters.zh: 他们使穷人离开正道；世上的贫民尽都隐藏。
chapters.en: They turn the needy out of the way: the poor of the earth hide themselves together.

chapters.zh: 这些贫穷人如同野驴出到旷野，殷勤寻找食物；他们靠著野地给儿女糊口，
chapters.en: Behold, wild asses in the desert, go they forth to their work; rising betimes for a prey: the wilderness food for them for children.

chapters.zh: 收割别人田间的禾稼，摘取恶人余剩的葡萄，
chapters.en: They reap his corn in the field: and they gather the vintage of the wicked.

chapters.zh: 终夜赤身无衣，天气寒冷毫无遮盖，
chapters.en: They cause the naked to lodge without clothing, that no covering in the cold.

chapters.zh: 在山上被大雨淋湿，因没有避身之处就挨近磐石。
chapters.en: They are wet with the showers of the mountains, and embrace the rock for want of a shelter.

chapters.zh: 又有人从母怀中抢夺孤儿，强取穷人的衣服为当头，
chapters.en: They pluck the fatherless from the breast, and take a pledge of the poor.

chapters.zh: 使人赤身无衣，到处流行，且因饥饿扛抬禾捆，
chapters.en: They cause to go naked without clothing, and they take away the sheaf the hungry;

chapters.zh: 在那些人的围墙内造油，醡酒，自己还口渴。
chapters.en: make oil within their walls, tread winepresses, and suffer thirst.

chapters.zh: 在多民的城内有人唉哼，受伤的人哀号；神却不理会那恶人的愚妄。
chapters.en: Men groan from out of the city, and the soul of the wounded crieth out: yet God layeth not folly .

chapters.zh: 又有人背弃光明，不认识光明的道，不住在光明的路上。
chapters.en: They are of those that rebel against the light; they know not the ways thereof, nor abide in the paths thereof.

chapters.zh: 杀人的黎明起来，杀害困苦穷乏人，夜间又作盗贼。
chapters.en: The murderer rising with the light killeth the poor and needy, and in the night is as a thief.

chapters.zh: 奸夫等候黄昏，说：必无眼能见我，就把脸蒙蔽。
chapters.en: The eye also of the adulterer waiteth for the twilight, saying, No eye shall see me: and disguiseth face.

chapters.zh: 盗贼黑夜挖窟窿；白日躲藏，并不认识光明。
chapters.en: In the dark they dig through houses, they had marked for themselves in the daytime: they know not the light.

chapters.zh: 他们看早晨如幽暗，因为他们晓得幽暗的惊骇。
