chapters.en: For the morning to them even as the shadow of death: if know the terrors of the shadow of death.

chapters.zh: 这些恶人犹如浮萍快快飘去。他们所得的分在世上被咒诅；他们不得再走葡萄园的路。
chapters.en: He swift as the waters; their portion is cursed in the earth: he beholdeth not the way of the vineyards.

chapters.zh: 干旱炎热消没雪水；阴间也如此消没犯罪之辈。
chapters.en: Drought and heat consume the snow waters: the grave have sinned.

chapters.zh: 怀他的母（原文是胎）要忘记他；虫子要吃他，觉得甘甜；他不再被人记念。不义的人必如树折断。
chapters.en: The womb shall forget him; the worm shall feed sweetly on him; he shall be no more remembered; and wickedness shall be broken as a tree.

chapters.zh: 他恶待（或译：他吞灭）不怀孕不生养的妇人，不善待寡妇。
chapters.en: He evil entreateth the barren beareth not: and doeth not good to the widow.

chapters.zh: 然而神用能力保全有势力的人；那性命难保的人仍然兴起。
chapters.en: He draweth also the mighty with his power: he riseth up, and no is sure of life.

chapters.zh: 神使他们安稳，他们就有所倚靠；神的眼目也看顾他们的道路。
chapters.en: it be given him in safety, whereon he resteth; yet his eyes upon their ways.

chapters.zh: 他们被高举，不过片时就没有了；他们降为卑，被除灭，与众人一样，又如谷穗被割。
chapters.en: They are exalted for a little while, but are gone and brought low; they are taken out of the way as all , and cut off as the tops of the ears of corn.

chapters.zh: 若不是这样，谁能证实我是说谎的，将我的言语驳为虚空呢？
chapters.en: And if not now, who will make me a liar, and make my speech nothing worth?

chapters.zh: 书亚人比勒达回答说：
chapters.en: Then answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,

chapters.zh: 神有治理之权，有威严可畏；他在高处施行和平。
chapters.en: Dominion and fear with him, he maketh peace in his high places.

chapters.zh: 他的诸军岂能数算？他的光亮一发，谁不蒙照呢？
chapters.en: Is there any number of his armies? and upon whom doth not his light arise?

chapters.zh: 这样在神面前，人怎能称义？妇人所生的怎能洁净？
chapters.en: How then can man be justified with God? or how can he be clean born of a woman?

chapters.zh: 在神眼前，月亮也无光亮，星宿也不清洁。
chapters.en: Behold even to the moon, and it shineth not; yea, the stars are not pure in his sight.

chapters.zh: 何况如虫的人，如蛆的世人呢！
chapters.en: How much less man, a worm? and the son of man, a worm?

chapters.zh: 约伯回答说：
chapters.en: But Job answered and said,

chapters.zh: 无能的人蒙你何等的帮助！膀臂无力的人蒙你何等的拯救！
chapters.en: How hast thou helped without power? savest thou the arm no strength?

chapters.zh: 无智慧的人蒙你何等的指教！你向他多显大知识！
chapters.en: How hast thou counselled no wisdom? and hast thou plentifully declared the thing as it is?

chapters.zh: 你向谁发出言语来？谁的灵从你而出？
chapters.en: To whom hast thou uttered words? and whose spirit came from thee?

chapters.zh: 在大水和水族以下的阴魂战兢。
chapters.en: Dead are formed from under the waters, and the inhabitants thereof.

chapters.zh: 在神面前，阴间显露；灭亡也不得遮掩。
chapters.en: Hell naked before him, and destruction hath no covering.

chapters.zh: 神将北极铺在空中，将大地悬在虚空；
chapters.en: He stretcheth out the north over the empty place, hangeth the earth upon nothing.

chapters.zh: 将水包在密云中，云却不破裂；
chapters.en: He bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds; and the cloud is not rent under them.

chapters.zh: 遮蔽他的宝座，将云铺在其上；
chapters.en: He holdeth back the face of his throne, spreadeth his cloud upon it.

chapters.zh: 在水面的周围划出界限，直到光明黑暗的交界。
chapters.en: He hath compassed the waters with bounds, until the day and night come to an end.

chapters.zh: 天的柱子因他的斥责震动惊奇。
chapters.en: The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at his reproof.

chapters.zh: 他以能力搅动（或译：平静）大海；他藉知识打伤拉哈伯，
chapters.en: He divideth the sea with his power, and by his understanding he smiteth through the proud.

chapters.zh: 藉他的灵使天有妆饰；他的手刺杀快蛇。
chapters.en: By his spirit he hath garnished the heavens; his hand hath formed the crooked serpent.

chapters.zh: 看哪，这不过是神工作的些微；我们所听于他的是何等细微的声音！他大能的雷声谁能明透呢？
chapters.en: Lo, these parts of his ways: but how little a portion is heard of him? but the thunder of his power who can understand?

chapters.zh: 约伯接著说：
chapters.en: Moreover Job continued his parable, and said,

chapters.zh: 神夺去我的理，全能者使我心中愁苦。我指著永生的神起誓：
chapters.en: God liveth, hath taken away my judgment; and the Almighty, hath vexed my soul;

chapters.zh: 我的生命尚在我里面；神所赐呼吸之气仍在我的鼻孔内。
chapters.en: All the while my breath in me, and the spirit of God in my nostrils;

chapters.zh: 我的嘴决不说非义之言；我的舌也不说诡诈之语。
chapters.en: My lips shall not speak wickedness, nor my tongue utter deceit.

chapters.zh: 我断不以你们为是；我至死必不以自己为不正！
chapters.en: God forbid that I should justify you: till I die I will not remove mine integrity from me.

chapters.zh: 我持定我的义，必不放松；在世的日子，我心必不责备我。
chapters.en: My righteousness I hold fast, and will not let it go: my heart shall not reproach so long as I live.

chapters.zh: 愿我的仇敌如恶人一样；愿那起来攻击我的，如不义之人一般。
chapters.en: Let mine enemy be as the wicked, and he that riseth up against me as the unrighteous.

chapters.zh: 不敬虔的人虽然得利，神夺取其命的时候还有甚么指望呢？
chapters.en: For what the hope of the hypocrite, though he hath gained, when God taketh away his soul?

chapters.zh: 患难临到他，神岂能听他的呼求？
chapters.en: Will God hear his cry when trouble cometh upon him?

chapters.zh: 他岂以全能者为乐，随时求告神呢？
chapters.en: Will he delight himself in the Almighty? will he always call upon God?

chapters.zh: 神的作为，我要指教你们；全能者所行的，我也不隐瞒。
chapters.en: I will teach you by the hand of God: which with the Almighty will I not conceal.

chapters.zh: 你们自己也都见过，为何全然变为虚妄呢？
chapters.en: Behold, all ye yourselves have seen ; why then are ye thus altogether vain?

chapters.zh: 神为恶人所定的分，强暴人从全能者所得的报（原文是产业）乃是这样：
chapters.en: This the portion of a wicked man with God, and the heritage of oppressors, they shall receive of the Almighty.

chapters.zh: 倘或他的儿女增多，还是被刀所杀；他的子孙必不得饱食。
chapters.en: If his children be multiplied, for the sword: and his offspring shall not be satisfied with bread.

chapters.zh: 他所遗留的人必死而埋葬；他的寡妇也不哀哭。
chapters.en: Those that remain of him shall be buried in death: and his widows shall not weep.

chapters.zh: 他虽积蓄银子如尘沙，预备衣服如泥土；
chapters.en: Though he heap up silver as the dust, and prepare raiment as the clay;

chapters.zh: 他只管预备，义人却要穿上；他的银子，无辜的人要分取。
chapters.en: He may prepare , but the just shall put on, and the innocent shall divide the silver.

chapters.zh: 他建造房屋如虫做窝，又如守望者所搭的棚。
chapters.en: He buildeth his house as a moth, and as a booth the keeper maketh.

chapters.zh: 他虽富足躺卧，却不得收殓，转眼之间就不在了。
chapters.en: The rich man shall lie down, but he shall not be gathered: he openeth his eyes, and he not.

chapters.zh: 惊恐如波涛将他追上；暴风在夜间将他刮去。
chapters.en: Terrors take hold on him as waters, a tempest stealeth him away in the night.

chapters.zh: 东风把他飘去，又刮他离开本处。
chapters.en: The east wind carrieth him away, and he departeth: and as a storm hurleth him out of his place.

chapters.zh: 神要向他射箭，并不留情；他恨不得逃脱神的手。
chapters.en: For shall cast upon him, and not spare: he would fain flee out of his hand.

chapters.zh: 人要向他拍掌，并要发叱声，使他离开本处。
chapters.en: shall clap their hands at him, and shall hiss him out of his place.

chapters.zh: 银子有矿；炼金有方。
chapters.en: Surely there is a vein for the silver, and a place for gold they fine .

chapters.zh: 铁从地里挖出；铜从石中镕化。
chapters.en: Iron is taken out of the earth, and brass molten the stone.

chapters.zh: 人为黑暗定界限，查究幽暗阴翳的石头，直到极处，
chapters.en: He setteth an end to darkness, and searcheth out all perfection: the stones of darkness, and the shadow of death.

chapters.zh: 在无人居住之处刨开矿穴，过路的人也想不到他们；又与人远离，悬在空中摇来摇去。
chapters.en: The flood breaketh out from the inhabitant; forgotten of the foot: they are dried up, they are gone away from men.

chapters.zh: 至于地，能出粮食，地内好像被火翻起来。
chapters.en: the earth, out of it cometh bread: and under it is turned up as it were fire.

chapters.zh: 地中的石头有蓝宝石，并有金沙。
chapters.en: The stones of it the place of sapphires: and it hath dust of gold.

chapters.zh: 矿中的路鸷鸟不得知道；鹰眼也未见过。
chapters.en: a path which no fowl knoweth, and which the vulture's eye hath not seen:

chapters.zh: 狂傲的野兽未曾行过；猛烈的狮子也未曾经过。
chapters.en: The lion's whelps have not trodden it, nor the fierce lion passed by it.

chapters.zh: 人伸手凿开坚石，倾倒山根，
chapters.en: He putteth forth his hand upon the rock; he overturneth the mountains by the roots.

chapters.zh: 在磐石中凿出水道，亲眼看见各样宝物。
chapters.en: He cutteth out rivers among the rocks; and his eye seeth every precious thing.

chapters.zh: 他封闭水不得滴流，使隐藏的物显露出来。
chapters.en: He bindeth the floods from overflowing; and hid bringeth he forth to light.

chapters.zh: 然而，智慧有何处可寻？聪明之处在哪里呢？
chapters.en: But where shall wisdom be found? and where the place of understanding?

chapters.zh: 智慧的价值无人能知，在活人之地也无处可寻。
chapters.en: Man knoweth not the price thereof; neither is it found in the land of the living.

chapters.zh: 深渊说：不在我内；沧海说：不在我中。
chapters.en: The depth saith, It not in me: and the sea saith, not with me.

chapters.zh: 智慧非用黄金可得，也不能平白银为他的价值。
chapters.en: It cannot be gotten for gold, neither shall silver be weighed the price thereof.

chapters.zh: 俄斐金和贵重的红玛瑙，并蓝宝石，不足与较量；
chapters.en: It cannot be valued with the gold of Ophir, with the precious onyx, or the sapphire.

chapters.zh: 黄金和玻璃不足与比较；精金的器皿不足与兑换。
chapters.en: The gold and the crystal cannot equal it: and the exchange of it jewels of fine gold.

chapters.zh: 珊瑚、水晶都不足论；智慧的价值胜过珍珠（或译：红宝石）。
chapters.en: No mention shall be made of coral, or of pearls: for the price of wisdom above rubies.

chapters.zh: 古实的红璧玺不足与比较；精金也不足与较量。
chapters.en: The topaz of Ethiopia shall not equal it, neither shall it be valued with pure gold.

chapters.zh: 智慧从何处来呢？聪明之处在哪里呢？
chapters.en: Whence then cometh wisdom? and where the place of understanding?

chapters.zh: 是向一切有生命的眼目隐藏，向空中的飞鸟掩蔽。
chapters.en: Seeing it is hid from the eyes of all living, and kept close from the fowls of the air.

chapters.zh: 灭没和死亡说：我们风闻其名。
chapters.en: Destruction and death say, We have heard the fame thereof with our ears.

chapters.zh: 神明白智慧的道路，晓得智慧的所在。
chapters.en: God understandeth the way thereof, and he knoweth the place thereof.

chapters.zh: 因他鉴察直到地极，遍观普天之下，
chapters.en: For he looketh to the ends of the earth, seeth under the whole heaven;

chapters.zh: 要为风定轻重，又度量诸水；
chapters.en: To make the weight for the winds; and he weigheth the waters by measure.

chapters.zh: 他为雨露定命令，为雷电定道路。
chapters.en: When he made a decree for the rain, and a way for the lightning of the thunder:

chapters.zh: 那时他看见智慧，而且述说；他坚定，并且查究。
chapters.en: Then did he see it, and declare it; he prepared it, yea, and searched it out.

chapters.zh: 他对人说：敬畏主就是智慧；远离恶便是聪明。
chapters.en: And unto man he said, Behold, the fear of the Lord, that wisdom; and to depart from evil understanding.

chapters.zh: 约伯又接著说：
chapters.en: Moreover Job continued his parable, and said,

chapters.zh: 惟愿我的景况如从前的月份，如神保守我的日子。
chapters.en: Oh that I were as months past, as the days God preserved me;

chapters.zh: 那时他的灯照在我头上；我藉他的光行过黑暗。
chapters.en: When his candle shined upon my head, by his light I walked darkness;

chapters.zh: 我愿如壮年的时候：那时我在帐棚中，神待我有密友之情；
chapters.en: As I was in the days of my youth, when the secret of God upon my tabernacle;

chapters.zh: 全能者仍与我同在；我的儿女都环绕我。
chapters.en: When the Almighty yet with me, my children about me;

chapters.zh: 奶多可洗我的脚；磐石为我出油成河。
chapters.en: When I washed my steps with butter, and the rock poured me out rivers of oil;

chapters.zh: 我出到城门，在街上设立座位；
chapters.en: When I went out to the gate through the city, I prepared my seat in the street!

chapters.zh: 少年人见我而回避，老年人也起身站立；
chapters.en: The young men saw me, and hid themselves: and the aged arose, stood up.

chapters.zh: 王子都停止说话，用手摀口；
chapters.en: The princes refrained talking, and laid hand on their mouth.

chapters.zh: 首领静默无声，舌头贴住上膛。
chapters.en: The nobles held their peace, and their tongue cleaved to the roof of their mouth.

chapters.zh: 耳朵听我的，就称我有福；眼睛看我的，便称赞我；
chapters.en: When the ear heard , then it blessed me; and when the eye saw , it gave witness to me:

chapters.zh: 因我拯救哀求的困苦人和无人帮助的孤儿。
chapters.en: Because I delivered the poor that cried, and the fatherless, and none to help him.

chapters.zh: 将要灭亡的为我祝福；我也使寡妇心中欢乐。
chapters.en: The blessing of him that was ready to perish came upon me: and I caused the widow's heart to sing for joy.

chapters.zh: 我以公义为衣服，以公平为外袍和冠冕。
chapters.en: I put on righteousness, and it clothed me: my judgment as a robe and a diadem.

chapters.zh: 我为瞎子的眼，瘸子的脚。
chapters.en: I was eyes to the blind, and feet I to the lame.

chapters.zh: 我为穷乏人的父；素不认识的人，我查明他的案件。
chapters.en: I a father to the poor: and the cause I knew not I searched out.

chapters.zh: 我打破不义之人的牙床，从他牙齿中夺了所抢的。
chapters.en: And I brake the jaws of the wicked, and plucked the spoil out of his teeth.

chapters.zh: 我便说：我必死在家中（原文是窝中），必增添我的日子，多如尘沙。
chapters.en: Then I said, I shall die in my nest, and I shall multiply days as the sand.

chapters.zh: 我的根长到水边；露水终夜沾在我的枝上。
chapters.en: My root spread out by the waters, and the dew lay all night upon my branch.

chapters.zh: 我的荣耀在身上增新；我的弓在手中日强。
chapters.en: My glory fresh in me, and my bow was renewed in my hand.

chapters.zh: 人听见我而仰望，静默等候我的指教。
chapters.en: Unto me gave ear, and waited, and kept silence at my counsel.

chapters.zh: 我说话之后，他们就不再说；我的言语像雨露滴在他们身上。
chapters.en: After my words they spake not again; and my speech dropped upon them.

chapters.zh: 他们仰望我如仰望雨，又张开口如切慕春雨。
chapters.en: And they waited for me as for the rain; and they opened their mouth wide for the latter rain.

chapters.zh: 他们不敢自信，我就向他们含笑；他们不使我脸上的光改变。
chapters.en: I laughed on them, they believed not; and the light of my countenance they cast not down.

chapters.zh: 我为他们选择道路，又坐首位；我如君王在军队中居住，又如吊丧的安慰伤心的人。
chapters.en: I chose out their way, and sat chief, and dwelt as a king in the army, as one comforteth the mourners.

chapters.zh: 但如今，比我年少的人戏笑我；其人之父我曾藐视，不肯安在看守我羊群的狗中。
chapters.en: But now younger than I have me in derision, whose fathers I would have disdained to have set with the dogs of my flock.

chapters.zh: 他们壮年的气力既已衰败，其手之力与我何益呢？
chapters.en: Yea, whereto the strength of their hands me, in whom old age was perished?

chapters.zh: 他们因穷乏饥饿，身体枯瘦，在荒废凄凉的幽暗中，龈干燥之地，
chapters.en: For want and famine solitary; fleeing into the wilderness in former time desolate and waste.

chapters.zh: 在草丛之中采咸草，罗腾（小树名，松类）的根为他们的食物。
chapters.en: Who cut up mallows by the bushes, and juniper roots their meat.

chapters.zh: 他们从人中被赶出；人追喊他们如贼一般，
chapters.en: They were driven forth from among , (they cried after them as a thief;)

chapters.zh: 以致他们住在荒谷之间，在地洞和岩穴中；
chapters.en: To dwell in the clifts of the valleys, caves of the earth, and the rocks.

chapters.zh: 在草丛中叫唤，在荆棘下聚集。
chapters.en: Among the bushes they brayed; under the nettles they were gathered together.

chapters.zh: 这都是愚顽下贱人的儿女；他们被鞭打，赶出境外。
chapters.en: children of fools, yea, children of base men: they were viler than the earth.

chapters.zh: 现在这些人以我为歌曲，以我为笑谈。
chapters.en: And now am I their song, yea, I am their byword.

chapters.zh: 他们厌恶我，躲在旁边站著，不住地吐唾沫在我脸上。
chapters.en: They abhor me, they flee far from me, and spare not to spit in my face.

chapters.zh: 松开他们的绳索苦待我，在我面前脱去辔头。
chapters.en: Because he hath loosed my cord, and afflicted me, they have also let loose the bridle before me.

chapters.zh: 这等下流人在我右边起来，推开我的脚，筑成战路来攻击我。
chapters.en: Upon right rise the youth; they push away my feet, and they raise up against me the ways of their destruction.

chapters.zh: 这些无人帮助的，毁坏我的道，加增我的灾。
chapters.en: They mar my path, they set forward my calamity, they have no helper.

chapters.zh: 他们来如同闯进大破口，在毁坏之间滚在我身上。
chapters.en: They came as a wide breaking in : in the desolation they rolled themselves .

chapters.zh: 惊恐临到我，驱逐我的尊荣如风；我的福禄如云过去。
chapters.en: Terrors are turned upon me: they pursue my soul as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloud.

chapters.zh: 现在我心极其悲伤；困苦的日子将我抓住。
chapters.en: And now my soul is poured out upon me; the days of affliction have taken hold upon me.

chapters.zh: 夜间，我里面的骨头刺我，疼痛不止，好像龈我。
chapters.en: My bones are pierced in me in the night season: and my sinews take no rest.

chapters.zh: 因神的大力，我的外衣污秽不堪，又如里衣的领子将我缠住。
chapters.en: By the great force is my garment changed: it bindeth me about as the collar of my coat.

chapters.zh: 神把我扔在淤泥中，我就像尘土和炉灰一般。
chapters.en: He hath cast me into the mire, and I am become like dust and ashes.

chapters.zh: 主啊，我呼求你，你不应允我；我站起来，你就定睛看我。
chapters.en: I cry unto thee, and thou dost not hear me: I stand up, and thou regardest me .

chapters.zh: 你向我变心，待我残忍，又用大能追逼我，
chapters.en: Thou art become cruel to me: with thy strong hand thou opposest thyself against me.

chapters.zh: 把我提在风中，使我驾风而行，又使我消灭在烈风中。
chapters.en: Thou liftest me up to the wind; thou causest me to ride , and dissolvest my substance.

chapters.zh: 我知道要使我临到死地，到那为众生所定的阴宅。
chapters.en: For I know thou wilt bring me death, and the house appointed for all living.

chapters.zh: 然而，人仆倒岂不伸手？遇灾难岂不求救呢？
chapters.en: Howbeit he will not stretch out hand to the grave, though they cry in his destruction.

chapters.zh: 人遭难，我岂不为他哭泣呢？人穷乏，我岂不为他忧愁呢？
chapters.en: Did not I weep for him that was in trouble? was my soul grieved for the poor?

chapters.zh: 我仰望得好处，灾祸就到了；我等待光明，黑暗便来了。
chapters.en: When I looked for good, then evil came : and when I waited for light, there came darkness.

chapters.zh: 我心里烦扰不安，困苦的日子临到我身。
chapters.en: My bowels boiled, and rested not: the days of affliction prevented me.

chapters.zh: 我没有日光就哀哭行去（或译：我面发黑并非因日晒）；我在会中站著求救。
chapters.en: I went mourning without the sun: I stood up, I cried in the congregation.

chapters.zh: 我与野狗为弟兄，与鸵鸟为同伴。
chapters.en: I am a brother to dragons, and a companion to owls.

chapters.zh: 我的皮肤黑而脱落；我的骨头因热烧焦。
chapters.en: My skin is black upon me, and my bones are burned with heat.

chapters.zh: 所以，我的琴音变为悲音；我的箫声变为哭声。
chapters.en: My harp also is to mourning, and my organ into the voice of them that weep.

chapters.zh: 我与眼睛立约，怎能恋恋瞻望处女呢？
chapters.en: I made a covenant with mine eyes; why then should I think upon a maid?

chapters.zh: 从至上的神所得之分，从至高全能者所得之业是甚么呢？
chapters.en: For what portion of God from above? and inheritance of the Almighty from on high?

chapters.zh: 岂不是祸患临到不义的，灾害临到作孽的呢？
chapters.en: not destruction to the wicked? and a strange to the workers of iniquity?

chapters.zh: 神岂不是察看我的道路，数点我的脚步呢？
chapters.en: Doth not he see my ways, and count all my steps?

chapters.zh: 我若与虚谎同行，脚若追随诡诈；
chapters.en: If I have walked with vanity, or if my foot hath hasted to deceit;

chapters.zh: （我若被公道的天平称度，使神可以知道我的纯正；）
chapters.en: Let me be weighed in an even balance, that God may know mine integrity.

chapters.zh: 我的脚步若偏离正路，我的心若随著我的眼目，若有玷污粘在我手上；
chapters.en: If my step hath turned out of the way, and mine heart walked after mine eyes, and if any blot hath cleaved to mine hands;

chapters.zh: 就愿我所种的有别人吃，我田所产的被拔出来。
chapters.en: let me sow, and let another eat; yea, let my offspring be rooted out.

chapters.zh: 我若受迷惑，向妇人起淫念，在邻舍的门外蹲伏，
chapters.en: If mine heart have been deceived by a woman, or I have laid wait at my neighbour's door;

chapters.zh: 就愿我的妻子给别人推磨，别人也与他同室。
chapters.en: let my wife grind unto another, and let others bow down upon her.

chapters.zh: 因为这是大罪，是审判官当罚的罪孽。
chapters.en: For this an heinous crime; yea, it an iniquity the judges.

chapters.zh: 这本是火焚烧，直到毁灭，必拔除我所有的家产。
chapters.en: For it a fire consumeth to destruction, and would root out all mine increase.

chapters.zh: 我的仆婢与我争辩的时候，我若藐视不听他们的情节；
chapters.en: If I did despise the cause of my manservant or of my maidservant, when they contended with me;

chapters.zh: 神兴起，我怎样行呢？他察问，我怎样回答呢？
chapters.en: What then shall I do when God riseth up? and when he visiteth, what shall I answer him?

chapters.zh: 造我在腹中的，不也是造他么？将他与我抟在腹中的岂不是一位么？
chapters.en: Did not he that made me in the womb make him? and did not one fashion us in the womb?

chapters.zh: 我若不容贫寒人得其所愿，或叫寡妇眼中失望，
chapters.en: If I have withheld the poor from desire, or have caused the eyes of the widow to fail;

chapters.zh: 或独自吃我一点食物，孤儿没有与我同吃；
chapters.en: Or have eaten my morsel myself alone, and the fatherless hath not eaten thereof;

chapters.zh: （从幼年时孤儿与我同长，好像父子一样；我从出母腹就扶助寡妇。）
chapters.en: (For from my youth he was brought up with me, as a father, and I have guided her from my mother's womb;)

chapters.zh: 我若见人因无衣死亡，或见穷乏人身无遮盖；
chapters.en: If I have seen any perish for want of clothing, or any poor without covering;

chapters.zh: 我若不使他因我羊的毛得暖，为我祝福；
chapters.en: If his loins have not blessed me, and he were warmed with the fleece of my sheep;

chapters.zh: 我若在城门口见有帮助我的，举手攻击孤儿；
chapters.en: If I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless, when I saw my help in the gate:

chapters.zh: 情愿我的肩头从缺盆骨脱落，我的膀臂从羊矢骨折断。
chapters.en: let mine arm fall from my shoulder blade, and mine arm be broken from the bone.

chapters.zh: 因神降的灾祸使我恐惧；因他的威严，我不能妄为。
chapters.en: For destruction God a terror to me, and by reason of his highness I could not endure.

chapters.zh: 我若以黄金为指望，对精金说：你是我的倚靠；
chapters.en: If I have made gold my hope, or have said to the fine gold, my confidence;

chapters.zh: 我若因财物丰裕，因我手多得资财而欢喜；
chapters.en: If I rejoiced because my wealth great, and because mine hand had gotten much;

chapters.zh: 我若见太阳发光，明月行在空中，
chapters.en: If I beheld the sun when it shined, or the moon walking brightness;

chapters.zh: 心就暗暗被引诱，口便亲手；
chapters.en: And my heart hath been secretly enticed, or my mouth hath kissed my hand:

chapters.zh: 这也是审判官当罚的罪孽，又是我背弃在上的神。
chapters.en: This also an iniquity the judge: for I should have denied the God above.

chapters.zh: 我若见恨我的遭报就欢喜，见他遭灾便高兴；
chapters.en: If I rejoiced at the destruction of him that hated me, or lifted up myself when evil found him:

chapters.zh: （我没有容口犯罪，咒诅他的生命；）
chapters.en: Neither have I suffered my mouth to sin by wishing a curse to his soul.

chapters.zh: 若我帐棚的人未尝说，谁不以主人的食物吃饱呢？
chapters.en: If the men of my tabernacle said not, Oh that we had of his flesh! we cannot be satisfied.

chapters.zh: （从来我没有容客旅在街上住宿，却开门迎接行路的人；）
chapters.en: The stranger did not lodge in the street: I opened my doors to the traveller.

chapters.zh: 我若像亚当（或译：别人）遮掩我的过犯，将罪孽藏在怀中；
chapters.en: If I covered my transgressions as Adam, by hiding mine iniquity in my bosom:

chapters.zh: 因惧怕大众，又因宗族藐视我使我惊恐，以致闭口无言，杜门不出；
chapters.en: Did I fear a great multitude, or did the contempt of families terrify me, that I kept silence, went not out of the door?

chapters.zh: 惟愿有一位肯听我！（看哪，在这里有我所划的押，愿全能者回答我！）
chapters.en: Oh that one would hear me! behold, my desire the Almighty would answer me, and mine adversary had written a book.

chapters.zh: 愿那敌我者所写的状词在我这里！我必带在肩上，又绑在头上为冠冕。
chapters.en: Surely I would take it upon my shoulder, bind it a crown to me.

chapters.zh: 我必向他述说我脚步的数目，必如君王进到他面前。
chapters.en: I would declare unto him the number of my steps; as a prince would I go near unto him.

chapters.zh: 我若夺取田地，这地向我喊冤，犁沟一同哭泣；
chapters.en: If my land cry against me, or that the furrows likewise thereof complain;

chapters.zh: 我若吃地的出产不给价值，或叫原主丧命；
chapters.en: If I have eaten the fruits thereof without money, or have caused the owners thereof to lose their life:

chapters.zh: 愿这地长蒺藜代替麦子，长恶草代替大麦。约伯的话说完了。
chapters.en: Let thistles grow instead of wheat, and cockle instead of barley. The words of Job are ended.

chapters.zh: 于是这三个人，因约伯自以为义就不再回答他。
chapters.en: So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he righteous in his own eyes.

chapters.zh: 那时有布西人兰族巴拉迦的儿子以利户向约伯发怒；因约伯自以为义，不以神为义。
chapters.en: Then was kindled the wrath of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the kindred of Ram: against Job was his wrath kindled, because he justified himself rather than God.

chapters.zh: 他又向约伯的三个朋友发怒；因为他们想不出回答的话来，仍以约伯为有罪。
chapters.en: Also against his three friends was his wrath kindled, because they had found no answer, and had condemned Job.

chapters.zh: 以利户要与约伯说话，就等候他们，因为他们比自己年老。
chapters.en: Now Elihu had waited till Job had spoken, because they elder than he.

chapters.zh: 以利户见这三个人口中无话回答，就怒气发作。
chapters.en: When Elihu saw that no answer in the mouth of three men, then his wrath was kindled.

chapters.zh: 布西人巴拉迦的儿子以利户回答说：我年轻，你们老迈；因此我退让，不敢向你们陈说我的意见。
chapters.en: And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said, I young, and ye very old; wherefore I was afraid, and durst not shew you mine opinion.

chapters.zh: 我说，年老的当先说话；寿高的当以智慧 . 教训人。
chapters.en: I said, Days should speak, and multitude of years should teach wisdom.

chapters.zh: 但在人里面有灵；全能者的气使人有聪明。
chapters.en: But a spirit in man: and the inspiration of the Almighty giveth them understanding.

chapters.zh: 尊贵的不都有智慧；寿高的不都能明白公平。
chapters.en: Great men are not wise: neither do the aged understand judgment.

chapters.zh: 因此我说：你们要听我言；我也要陈说我的意见。
chapters.en: Therefore I said, Hearken to me; I also will shew mine opinion.

chapters.zh: 你们查究所要说的话；那时我等候你们的话，侧耳听你们的辩论，
chapters.en: Behold, I waited for your words; I gave ear to your reasons, whilst ye searched out what to say.

chapters.zh: 留心听你们；谁知你们中间无一人折服约伯，驳倒他的话。
chapters.en: Yea, I attended unto you, and, behold, none of you that convinced Job, that answered his words:

chapters.zh: 你们切不可说：我们寻得智慧；神能胜他，人却不能。
chapters.en: Lest ye should say, We have found out wisdom: God thrusteth him down, not man.

chapters.zh: 约伯没有向我争辩；我也不用你们的话回答他。
chapters.en: Now he hath not directed words against me: neither will I answer him with your speeches.

chapters.zh: 他们惊奇不再回答，一言不发。
chapters.en: They were amazed, they answered no more: they left off speaking.

chapters.zh: 我岂因他们不说话，站住不再回答，仍旧等候呢？
chapters.en: When I had waited, (for they spake not, but stood still, answered no more;)

chapters.zh: 我也要回答我的一分话，陈说我的意见。
chapters.en: , I will answer also my part, I also will shew mine opinion.

chapters.zh: 因为我的言语满怀；我里面的灵激动我。
chapters.en: For I am full of matter, the spirit within me constraineth me.

chapters.zh: 我的胸怀如盛酒之囊没有出气之缝，又如新皮袋快要破裂。
chapters.en: Behold, my belly as wine hath no vent; it is ready to burst like new bottles.

chapters.zh: 我要说话，使我舒畅；我要开口回答。
chapters.en: I will speak, that I may be refreshed: I will open my lips and answer.

chapters.zh: 我必不看人的情面，也不奉承人。
chapters.en: Let me not, I pray you, accept any man's person, neither let me give flattering titles unto man.

chapters.zh: 我不晓得奉承；若奉承，造我的主必快快除灭我。
chapters.en: For I know not to give flattering titles; my maker would soon take me away.

chapters.zh: 约伯啊，请听我的话，留心听我一切的言语。
chapters.en: Wherefore, Job, I pray thee, hear my speeches, and hearken to all my words.

chapters.zh: 我现在开口，用舌发言。
chapters.en: Behold, now I have opened my mouth, my tongue hath spoken in my mouth.

chapters.zh: 我的言语要发明心中所存的正直；我所知道的，我嘴唇要诚实地说出。
chapters.en: My words the uprightness of my heart: and my lips shall utter knowledge clearly.

chapters.zh: 神的灵造我；全能者的气使我得生。
chapters.en: The Spirit of God hath made me, and the breath of the Almighty hath given me life.

chapters.zh: 你若回答我，就站起来，在我面前陈明。
chapters.en: If thou canst answer me, set in order before me, stand up.

chapters.zh: 我在神面前与你一样，也是用土造成。
chapters.en: Behold, I according to thy wish in God's stead: I also am formed out of the clay.

chapters.zh: 我不用威严惊吓你，也不用势力重压你。
chapters.en: Behold, my terror shall not make thee afraid, neither shall my hand be heavy upon thee.

chapters.zh: 你所说的，我听见了，也听见你的言语，说：
chapters.en: Surely thou hast spoken in mine hearing, and I have heard the voice of words, ,

chapters.zh: 我是清洁无过的，我是无辜的；在我里面也没有罪孽。
chapters.en: I am clean without transgression, I innocent; neither iniquity in me.

chapters.zh: 神找机会攻击我，以我为仇敌，
chapters.en: Behold, he findeth occasions against me, he counteth me for his enemy,

chapters.zh: 把我的脚上了木狗，窥察我一切的道路。
chapters.en: He putteth my feet in the stocks, he marketh all my paths.

chapters.zh: 我要回答你说：你这话无理，因神比世人更大。
chapters.en: Behold, this thou art not just: I will answer thee, that God is greater than man.

chapters.zh: 你为何与他争论呢？因他的事都不对人解说？
chapters.en: Why dost thou strive against him? for he giveth not account of any of his matters.

chapters.zh: 神说一次、两次，世人却不理会。
chapters.en: For God speaketh once, yea twice, perceiveth it not.

chapters.zh: 人躺在床上沉睡的时候，神就用梦和夜间的异象，
chapters.en: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falleth upon men, in slumberings upon the bed;

chapters.zh: 开通他们的耳朵，将当受的教训印在他们心上，
chapters.en: Then he openeth the ears of men, and sealeth their instruction,

chapters.zh: 好叫人不从自己的谋算，不行骄傲的事（原文是将骄傲向人隐藏），
chapters.en: That he may withdraw man purpose, and hide pride from man.

chapters.zh: 拦阻人不陷于坑里，不死在刀下。
chapters.en: He keepeth back his soul from the pit, and his life from perishing by the sword.

chapters.zh: 人在床上被惩治，骨头中不住地疼痛，
chapters.en: He is chastened also with pain upon his bed, and the multitude of his bones with strong :

chapters.zh: 以致他的口厌弃食物，心厌恶美味。
chapters.en: So that his life abhorreth bread, and his soul dainty meat.

chapters.zh: 他的肉消瘦，不得再见；先前不见的骨头都凸出来。
chapters.en: His flesh is consumed away, that it cannot be seen; and his bones were not seen stick out.

chapters.zh: 他的灵魂临近深坑；他的生命近于灭命的。
chapters.en: Yea, his soul draweth near unto the grave, and his life to the destroyers.

chapters.zh: 一千天使中，若有一个作传话的与神同在，指示人所当行的事，
chapters.en: If there be a messenger with him, an interpreter, one among a thousand, to shew unto man his uprightness:

chapters.zh: 神就给他开恩，说：救赎他免得下坑；我已经得了赎价。
chapters.en: Then he is gracious unto him, and saith, Deliver him from going down to the pit: I have found a ransom.

chapters.zh: 他的肉要比孩童的肉更嫩；他就返老还童。
chapters.en: His flesh shall be fresher than a child's: he shall return to the days of his youth:

chapters.zh: 他祷告神，神就喜悦他，使他欢呼朝见神的面；神又看他为义。
chapters.en: He shall pray unto God, and he will be favourable unto him: and he shall see his face with joy: for he will render unto man his righteousness.

chapters.zh: 他在人前歌唱说：我犯了罪，颠倒是非，这竟与我无益。
chapters.en: He looketh upon men, and say, I have sinned, and perverted right, and it profited me not;

chapters.zh: 神救赎我的灵魂免入深坑；我的生命也必见光。
chapters.en: He will deliver his soul from going into the pit, and his life shall see the light.

chapters.zh: 神两次、三次向人行这一切的事，
chapters.en: Lo, all these worketh God oftentimes with man,

chapters.zh: 为要从深坑救回人的灵魂，使他被光照耀，与活人一样。
chapters.en: To bring back his soul from the pit, to be enlightened with the light of the living.

chapters.zh: 约伯啊，你当侧耳听我的话，不要作声，等我讲说。
chapters.en: Mark well, O Job, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I will speak.

chapters.zh: 你若有话说，就可以回答我；你只管说，因我愿以你为是。
chapters.en: If thou hast any thing to say, answer me: speak, for I desire to justify thee.

chapters.zh: 若不然，你就听我说；你不要作声，我便将智慧教训你。
chapters.en: If not, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I shall teach thee wisdom.

chapters.zh: 以利户又说：
chapters.en: Furthermore Elihu answered and said,

chapters.zh: 你们智慧人要听我的话；有知识的人要留心听我说。
chapters.en: Hear my words, O ye wise ; and give ear unto me, ye that have knowledge.

chapters.zh: 因为耳朵试验话语，好像上膛尝食物。
chapters.en: For the ear trieth words, as the mouth tasteth meat.

chapters.zh: 我们当选择何为是，彼此知道何为善。
chapters.en: Let us choose to us judgment: let us know among ourselves what good.

chapters.zh: 约伯曾说：我是公义，神夺去我的理；
chapters.en: For Job hath said, I am righteous: and God hath taken away my judgment.

chapters.zh: 我虽有理，还算为说谎言的；我虽无过，受的伤还不能医治。
chapters.en: Should I lie against my right? my wound incurable without transgression.

chapters.zh: 谁像约伯，喝讥诮如同喝水呢？
chapters.en: What man like Job, drinketh up scorning like water?

chapters.zh: 他与作孽的结伴，和恶人同行。
chapters.en: Which goeth in company with the workers of iniquity, and walketh with wicked men.

chapters.zh: 他说：人以神为乐，总是无益。
chapters.en: For he hath said, It profiteth a man nothing that he should delight himself with God.

chapters.zh: 所以，你们明理的人要听我的话。神断不致行恶；全能者断不致作孽。
chapters.en: Therefore hearken unto me, ye men of understanding: far be it from God, wickedness; and the Almighty, iniquity.

chapters.zh: 他必按人所做的报应人，使各人照所行的得报。
chapters.en: For the work of a man shall he render unto him, and cause every man to find according to ways.

chapters.zh: 神必不作恶；全能者也不偏离公平。
chapters.en: Yea, surely God will not do wickedly, neither will the Almighty pervert judgment.

chapters.zh: 谁派他治理地，安定全世界呢？
chapters.en: Who hath given him a charge over the earth? or who hath disposed the whole world?

chapters.zh: 他若专心为己，将灵和气收归自己，
chapters.en: If he set his heart upon man, he gather unto himself his spirit and his breath;

chapters.zh: 凡有血气的就必一同死亡；世人必仍归尘土。
chapters.en: All flesh shall perish together, and man shall turn again unto dust.

chapters.zh: 你若明理，就当听我的话，留心听我言语的声音。
chapters.en: If now understanding, hear this: hearken to the voice of my words.

chapters.zh: 难道恨恶公平的可以掌权么？那有公义的、有大能的，岂可定他有罪么？
chapters.en: Shall even he that hateth right govern? and wilt thou condemn him that is most just?

chapters.zh: 他对君王说：你是鄙陋的；对贵臣说：你是邪恶的。
chapters.en: to say to a king, wicked? to princes, ungodly?

chapters.zh: 他待王子不徇情面，也不看重富足的过于贫穷的，因为都是他手所造。
chapters.en: that accepteth not the persons of princes, nor regardeth the rich more than the poor? for they all the work of his hands.

chapters.zh: 在转眼之间，半夜之中，他们就死亡。百姓被震动而去世；有权力的被夺去非借人手。
chapters.en: In a moment shall they die, and the people shall be troubled at midnight, and pass away: and the mighty shall be taken away without hand.

chapters.zh: 神注目观看人的道路，看明人的脚步。
chapters.en: For his eyes upon the ways of man, and he seeth all his goings.

chapters.zh: 没有黑暗、阴翳能给作孽的藏身。
chapters.en: no darkness, nor shadow of death, where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.

chapters.zh: 神审判人，不必使人到他面前再三鉴察。
chapters.en: For he will not lay upon man more ; that he should enter into judgment with God.

chapters.zh: 他用难测之法打破有能力的人，设立别人代替他们。
chapters.en: He shall break in pieces mighty men without number, and set others in their stead.

chapters.zh: 他原知道他们的行为，使他们在夜间倾倒灭亡。
chapters.en: Therefore he knoweth their works, and he overturneth in the night, so that they are destroyed.

chapters.zh: 他在众人眼前击打他们，如同击打恶人一样。
chapters.en: He striketh them as wicked men in the open sight of others;

chapters.zh: 因为他们偏行不跟从他，也不留心他的道，
chapters.en: Because they turned back from him, and would not consider any of his ways:

chapters.zh: 甚至使贫穷人的哀声达到他那里；他也听了困苦人的哀声。
chapters.en: So that they cause the cry of the poor to come unto him, and he heareth the cry of the afflicted.

chapters.zh: 他使人安静，谁能扰乱（或译：定罪）呢？他掩面，谁能见他呢？无论待一国或一人都是如此 ─
chapters.en: When he giveth quietness, who then can make trouble? and when he hideth face, who then can behold him? whether against a nation, or against a man only:

chapters.zh: 使不虔敬的人不得作王，免得有人牢笼百姓。
chapters.en: That the hypocrite reign not, lest the people be ensnared.

chapters.zh: 有谁对神说：我受了责罚，不再犯罪；
chapters.en: Surely it is meet to be said unto God, I have borne , I will not offend :

chapters.zh: 我所看不明的，求你指教我；我若作了孽，必不再作？
chapters.en: I see not teach thou me: if I have done iniquity, I will do no more.

chapters.zh: 他施行报应，岂要随你的心愿、叫你推辞不受么？选定的是你，不是我。你所知道的只管说罢！
chapters.en: according to thy mind? he will recompense it, whether thou refuse, or whether thou choose; and not I: therefore speak what thou knowest.

chapters.zh: 明理的人和听我话的智慧人必对我说：
chapters.en: Let men of understanding tell me, and let a wise man hearken unto me.

chapters.zh: 约伯说话没有知识，言语中毫无智慧。
chapters.en: Job hath spoken without knowledge, and his words without wisdom.

chapters.zh: 愿约伯被试验到底，因他回答像恶人一样。
chapters.en: My desire Job may be tried unto the end because of answers for wicked men.

chapters.zh: 他在罪上又加悖逆；在我们中间拍手，用许多言语轻慢神。
chapters.en: For he addeth rebellion unto his sin, he clappeth among us, and multiplieth his words against God.

chapters.zh: 以利户又说：
chapters.en: Elihu spake moreover, and said,

chapters.zh: 你以为有理，或以为你的公义胜于神的公义，
chapters.en: Thinkest thou this to be right, thou saidst, My righteousness more than God's?

chapters.zh: 才说这与我有甚么益处？我不犯罪比犯罪有甚么好处呢？
chapters.en: For thou saidst, What advantage will it be unto thee? , What profit shall I have, from my sin?

chapters.zh: 我要回答你和在你这里的朋友。
chapters.en: I will answer thee, and thy companions with thee.

chapters.zh: 你要向天观看，瞻望那高于你的穹苍。
chapters.en: Look unto the heavens, and see; and behold the clouds are higher than thou.

chapters.zh: 你若犯罪，能使神受何害呢？你的过犯加增，能使神受何损呢？
chapters.en: If thou sinnest, what doest thou against him? or thy transgressions be multiplied, what doest thou unto him?

chapters.zh: 你若是公义，还能加增他甚么呢？他从你手里还接受甚么呢？
chapters.en: If thou be righteous, what givest thou him? or what receiveth he of thine hand?

chapters.zh: 你的过恶或能害你这类的人；你的公义或能叫世人得益处。
chapters.en: Thy wickedness a man as thou ; and thy righteousness the son of man.

chapters.zh: 人因多受欺压就哀求，因受能者的辖制（原文是膀臂）便求救，
chapters.en: By reason of the multitude of oppressions they make to cry: they cry out by reason of the arm of the mighty.

chapters.zh: 却无人说：造我的神在哪里？他使人夜间歌唱，
chapters.en: But none saith, Where God my maker, who giveth songs in the night;

chapters.zh: 教训我们胜于地上的走兽，使我们有聪明胜于空中的飞鸟。
chapters.en: Who teacheth us more than the beasts of the earth, and maketh us wiser than the fowls of heaven?

chapters.zh: 他们在那里，因恶人的骄傲呼求，却无人答应。
chapters.en: There they cry, but none giveth answer, because of the pride of evil men.

chapters.zh: 虚妄的呼求，神必不垂听；全能者也必不眷顾。
chapters.en: Surely God will not hear vanity, neither will the Almighty regard it.

chapters.zh: 何况你说，你不得见他；你的案件在他面前，你等候他罢。
chapters.en: Although thou sayest thou shalt not see him, judgment before him; therefore trust thou in him.

chapters.zh: 但如今因他未曾发怒降罚，也不甚理会狂傲，
chapters.en: But now, because not , he hath visited in his anger; yet he knoweth not in great extremity:

chapters.zh: 所以约伯开口说虚妄的话，多发无知识的言语。
chapters.en: Therefore doth Job open his mouth in vain; he multiplieth words without knowledge.

chapters.zh: 以利户又接著说：
chapters.en: Elihu also proceeded, and said,

chapters.zh: 你再容我片时，我就指示你，因我还有话为神说。
chapters.en: Suffer me a little, and I will shew thee that yet to speak on God's behalf.

chapters.zh: 我要将所知道的从远处引来，将公义归给造我的主。
chapters.en: I will fetch my knowledge from afar, and will ascribe righteousness to my Maker.

chapters.zh: 我的言语真不虚谎；有知识全备的与你同在。
chapters.en: For truly my words not false: he that is perfect in knowledge with thee.

chapters.zh: 神有大能，并不藐视人；他的智慧甚广。
chapters.en: Behold, God mighty, and despiseth not mighty in strength wisdom.

chapters.zh: 他不保护恶人的性命，却为困苦人伸冤。
chapters.en: He preserveth not the life of the wicked: but giveth right to the poor.

chapters.zh: 他时常看顾义人，使他们和君王同坐宝座，永远要被高举。
chapters.en: He withdraweth not his eyes from the righteous: but with kings on the throne; yea, he doth establish them for ever, and they are exalted.

chapters.zh: 他们若被锁炼捆住，被苦难的绳索缠住，
chapters.en: And if bound in fetters, be holden in cords of affliction;

chapters.zh: 他就把他们的作为和过犯指示他们，叫他们知道有骄傲的行动。
chapters.en: Then he sheweth them their work, and their transgressions that they have exceeded.

chapters.zh: 他也开通他们的耳朵得受教训，吩咐他们离开罪孽转回。
chapters.en: He openeth also their ear to discipline, and commandeth that they return from iniquity.

chapters.zh: 他们若听从事奉他，就必度日亨通，历年福乐；
chapters.en: If they obey and serve , they shall spend their days in prosperity, and their years in pleasures.

chapters.zh: 若不听从，就要被刀杀灭，无知无识而死。
chapters.en: But if they obey not, they shall perish by the sword, and they shall die without knowledge.

chapters.zh: 那心中不敬虔的人积蓄怒气；神捆绑他们，他们竟不求救；
chapters.en: But the hypocrites in heart heap up wrath: they cry not when he bindeth them.

chapters.zh: 必在青年时死亡，与污秽人一样丧命。
chapters.en: They die in youth, and their life among the unclean.

chapters.zh: 神藉著困苦救拔困苦人，趁他们受欺压开通他们的耳朵。
chapters.en: He delivereth the poor in his affliction, and openeth their ears in oppression.

chapters.zh: 神也必引你出离患难，进入宽阔不狭窄之地；摆在你席上的必满有肥甘。
chapters.en: Even so would he have removed thee out of the strait a broad place, where no straitness; and that which should be set on thy table full of fatness.

chapters.zh: 但你满口有恶人批评的言语；判断和刑罚抓住你。
chapters.en: But thou hast fulfilled the judgment of the wicked: judgment and justice take hold .

chapters.zh: 不可容忿怒触动你，使你不服责罚；也不可因赎价大就偏行。
chapters.en: Because wrath, lest he take thee away with stroke: then a great ransom cannot deliver thee.

chapters.zh: 你的呼求（或译：资财），或是你一切的势力，果有灵验，叫你不受患难么？
chapters.en: Will he esteem thy riches? , not gold, nor all the forces of strength.

chapters.zh: 不要切慕黑夜，就是众民在本处被除灭的时候。
chapters.en: Desire not the night, when people are cut off in their place.

chapters.zh: 你要谨慎，不可重看罪孽，因你选择罪孽过于选择苦难。
chapters.en: Take heed, regard not iniquity: for this hast thou chosen rather than affliction.

chapters.zh: 神行事有高大的能力；教训人的有谁像他呢？
chapters.en: Behold, God exalteth by his power: who teacheth like him?

chapters.zh: 谁派定他的道路？谁能说：你所行的不义？
chapters.en: Who hath enjoined him his way? or who can say, Thou hast wrought iniquity?

chapters.zh: 你不可忘记称赞他所行的为大，就是人所歌颂的。
chapters.en: Remember that thou magnify his work, which men behold.

chapters.zh: 他所行的，万人都看见；世人也从远处观看。
chapters.en: Every man may see it; man may behold afar off.

chapters.zh: 神为大，我们不能全知；他的年数不能测度。
chapters.en: Behold, God great, and we know not, neither can the number of his years be searched out.

chapters.zh: 他吸取水点，这水点从云雾中就变成雨；
chapters.en: For he maketh small the drops of water: they pour down rain according to the vapour thereof:

chapters.zh: 云彩将雨落下，沛然降与世人。
chapters.en: Which the clouds do drop distil upon man abundantly.

chapters.zh: 谁能明白云彩如何铺张，和神行宫的雷声呢？
chapters.en: Also can understand the spreadings of the clouds, the noise of his tabernacle?

chapters.zh: 他将亮光普照在自己的四围；他又遮覆海底。
chapters.en: Behold, he spreadeth his light upon it, and covereth the bottom of the sea.

chapters.zh: 他用这些审判众民，且赐丰富的粮食。
chapters.en: For by them judgeth he the people; he giveth meat in abundance.

chapters.zh: 他以电光遮手，命闪电击中敌人（或译：中了靶子）。
chapters.en: With clouds he covereth the light; and commandeth it by that cometh betwixt.

chapters.zh: 所发的雷声显明他的作为，又向牲畜指明要起暴风。
chapters.en: The noise thereof sheweth concerning it, the cattle also concerning the vapour.

chapters.zh: 因此我心战兢，从原处移动。
chapters.en: At this also my heart trembleth, and is moved out of his place.

chapters.zh: 听啊，神轰轰的声音是他口中所发的响声。
chapters.en: Hear attentively the noise of his voice, and the sound goeth out of his mouth.

chapters.zh: 他发响声震遍天下，发电光闪到地极。
chapters.en: He directeth it under the whole heaven, and his lightning unto the ends of the earth.

chapters.zh: 随后人听见有雷声轰轰，大发威严，雷电接连不断。
chapters.en: After it a voice roareth: he thundereth with the voice of his excellency; and he will not stay them when his voice is heard.

chapters.zh: 神发出奇妙的雷声；他行大事，我们不能测透。
chapters.en: God thundereth marvellously with his voice; great things doeth he, which we cannot comprehend.

chapters.zh: 他对雪说：要降在地上；对大雨和暴雨也是这样说。
chapters.en: For he saith to the snow, Be thou the earth; likewise to the small rain, and to the great rain of his strength.

chapters.zh: 他封住各人的手，叫所造的万人都晓得他的作为。
chapters.en: He sealeth up the hand of every man; that all men may know his work.

chapters.zh: 百兽进入穴中，卧在洞内。
chapters.en: Then the beasts go into dens, and remain in their places.

chapters.zh: 暴风出于南宫；寒冷出于北方。
chapters.en: Out of the south cometh the whirlwind: and cold out of the north.

chapters.zh: 神嘘气成冰；宽阔之水也都凝结。
chapters.en: By the breath of God frost is given: and the breadth of the waters is straitened.

chapters.zh: 他使密云盛满水气，布散电光之云；
chapters.en: Also by watering he wearieth the thick cloud: he scattereth his bright cloud:

chapters.zh: 这云是藉他的指引游行旋转，得以在全地面上行他一切所吩咐的，
chapters.en: And it is turned round about by his counsels: that they may do whatsoever he commandeth them upon the face of the world in the earth.

chapters.zh: 或为责罚，或为润地，或为施行慈爱。
chapters.en: He causeth it to come, whether for correction, or for his land, or for mercy.

chapters.zh: 约伯啊，你要留心听，要站立思想神奇妙的作为。
chapters.en: Hearken unto this, O Job: stand still, and consider the wondrous works of God.

chapters.zh: 神如何吩咐这些，如何使云中的电光照耀，你知道么？
chapters.en: Dost thou know when God disposed them, and caused the light of his cloud to shine?

chapters.zh: 云彩如何浮于空中，那知识全备者奇妙的作为，你知道么？
chapters.en: Dost thou know the balancings of the clouds, the wondrous works of him which is perfect in knowledge?

chapters.zh: 南风使地寂静，你的衣服就如火热，你知道么？
chapters.en: How thy garments warm, when he quieteth the earth by the south ?

chapters.zh: 你岂能与神同铺穹苍么？这穹苍坚硬，如同铸成的镜子。
chapters.en: Hast thou with him spread out the sky, strong, as a molten looking glass?

chapters.zh: 我们愚昧不能陈说；请你指教我们该对他说甚么话。
chapters.en: Teach us what we shall say unto him; we cannot order by reason of darkness.

chapters.zh: 人岂可说：我愿与他说话？岂有人自愿灭亡么？
chapters.en: Shall it be told him that I speak? if a man speak, surely he shall be swallowed up.

chapters.zh: 现在有云遮蔽，人不得见穹苍的光亮；但风吹过，天又发晴。
chapters.en: And now see not the bright light which in the clouds: but the wind passeth, and cleanseth them.

chapters.zh: 金光出于北方，在神那里有可怕的威严。
chapters.en: Fair weather cometh out of the north: with God terrible majesty.

chapters.zh: 论到全能者，我们不能测度；他大有能力，有公平和大义，必不苦待人。
chapters.en: the Almighty, we cannot find him out: excellent in power, and in judgment, and in plenty of justice: he will not afflict.

chapters.zh: 所以，人敬畏他；凡自以为心中有智慧的人，他都不顾念。
chapters.en: Men do therefore fear him: he respecteth not any wise of heart.

chapters.zh: 那时，耶和华从旋风中回答约伯说：
chapters.en: Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind, and said,

chapters.zh: 谁用无知的言语使我的旨意暗昧不明，
chapters.en: Who this that darkeneth counsel by words without knowledge?

chapters.zh: 你要如勇士束腰；我问你，你可以指示我。
chapters.en: Gird up now thy loins like a man; for I will demand of thee, and answer thou me.

chapters.zh: 我立大地根基的时候，你在哪里呢？你若有聪明，只管说罢！
chapters.en: Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth? declare, if thou hast understanding.

chapters.zh: 你若晓得就说，是谁定地的尺度？是谁把准绳拉在其上？
chapters.en: Who hath laid the measures thereof, if thou knowest? or who hath stretched the line upon it?

chapters.zh: 地的根基安置在何处？地的角石是谁安放的？
chapters.en: Whereupon are the foundations thereof fastened? or who laid the corner stone thereof;

chapters.zh: 那时，晨星一同歌唱；神的众子也都欢呼。
chapters.en: When the morning stars sang together, and all the sons of God shouted for joy?

chapters.zh: 海水冲出，如出胎胞，那时谁将他关闭呢？
chapters.en: Or shut up the sea with doors, when it brake forth, it had issued out of the womb?

chapters.zh: 是我用云彩当海的衣服，用幽暗当包裹他的布，
chapters.en: When I made the cloud the garment thereof, and thick darkness a swaddlingband for it,

chapters.zh: 为他定界限，又安门和闩，
chapters.en: And brake up for it my decreed , and set bars and doors,

chapters.zh: 说：你只可到这里，不可越过；你狂傲的浪要到此止住。
chapters.en: And said, Hitherto shalt thou come, but no further: and here shall thy proud waves be stayed?

chapters.zh: 你自生以来，曾命定晨光，使清晨的日光知道本位，
chapters.en: Hast thou commanded the morning since thy days; caused the dayspring to know his place;

chapters.zh: 叫这光普照地的四极，将恶人从其中驱逐出来么？
chapters.en: That it might take hold of the ends of the earth, that the wicked might be shaken out of it?

chapters.zh: 因这光，地面改变如泥上印印，万物出现如衣服一样。
chapters.en: It is turned as clay the seal; and they stand as a garment.

chapters.zh: 亮光不照恶人；强横的膀臂也必折断。
chapters.en: And from the wicked their light is withholden, and the high arm shall be broken.

chapters.zh: 你曾进到海源，或在深渊的隐密处行走么？
chapters.en: Hast thou entered into the springs of the sea? or hast thou walked in the search of the depth?

chapters.zh: 死亡的门曾向你显露么？死荫的门你曾见过么？
chapters.en: Have the gates of death been opened unto thee? or hast thou seen the doors of the shadow of death?

chapters.zh: 地的广大你能明透么？你若全知道，只管说罢！
chapters.en: Hast thou perceived the breadth of the earth? declare if thou knowest it all.

chapters.zh: 光明的居所从何而至？黑暗的本位在于何处？
chapters.en: Where the way light dwelleth? and darkness, where the place thereof,

chapters.zh: 你能带到本境，能看明其室之路么？
chapters.en: That thou shouldest take it to the bound thereof, and that thou shouldest know the paths the house thereof?

chapters.zh: 你总知道，因为你早已生在世上，你日子的数目也多。
chapters.en: Knowest thou , because thou wast then born? or the number of thy days great?

chapters.zh: 你曾进入雪库，或见过雹仓么？
chapters.en: Hast thou entered into the treasures of the snow? or hast thou seen the treasures of the hail,

chapters.zh: 这雪雹乃是我为降灾，并打仗和争战的日子所预备的。
chapters.en: Which I have reserved against the time of trouble, against the day of battle and war?

chapters.zh: 光亮从何路分开？东风从何路分散遍地？
chapters.en: By what way is the light parted, scattereth the east wind upon the earth?

chapters.zh: 谁为雨水分道？谁为雷电开路？
chapters.en: Who hath divided a watercourse for the overflowing of waters, or a way for the lightning of thunder;

chapters.zh: 使雨降在无人之地、无人居住的旷野？
chapters.en: To cause it to rain on the earth, no man the wilderness, wherein no man;

chapters.zh: 使荒废凄凉之地得以丰足，青草得以发生？
chapters.en: To satisfy the desolate and waste ; and to cause the bud of the tender herb to spring forth?

chapters.zh: 雨有父么？露水珠是谁生的呢？
chapters.en: Hath the rain a father? or who hath begotten the drops of dew?

chapters.zh: 冰出于谁的胎？天上的霜是谁生的呢？
chapters.en: Out of whose womb came the ice? and the hoary frost of heaven, who hath gendered it?

chapters.zh: 诸水坚硬（或译：隐藏）如石头；深渊之面凝结成冰。
chapters.en: The waters are hid as a stone, and the face of the deep is frozen.

chapters.zh: 你能系住昴星的结么？能解开参星的带么？
chapters.en: Canst thou bind the sweet influences of Pleiades, or loose the bands of Orion?

chapters.zh: 你能按时领出十二宫么？能引导北斗和随他的众星（星：原文是子）么？
chapters.en: Canst thou bring forth Mazzaroth in his season? or canst thou guide Arcturus with his sons?

chapters.zh: 你知道天的定例么？能使地归在天的权下么？
chapters.en: Knowest thou the ordinances of heaven? canst thou set the dominion thereof in the earth?

chapters.zh: 你能向云彩扬起声来，使倾盆的雨遮盖你么？
chapters.en: Canst thou lift up thy voice to the clouds, that abundance of waters may cover thee?

chapters.zh: 你能发出闪电，叫他行去，使他对你说：我们在这里？
chapters.en: Canst thou send lightnings, that they may go, and say unto thee, Here we ?

chapters.zh: 谁将智慧放在怀中？谁将聪明赐于心内？
chapters.en: Who hath put wisdom in the inward parts? or who hath given understanding to the heart?

chapters.zh: 谁能用智慧数算云彩呢？尘土聚集成团，土块紧紧结连；那时，谁能倾倒天上的瓶呢？
chapters.en: Who can number the clouds in wisdom? or who can stay the bottles of heaven,

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: When the dust groweth into hardness, and the clods cleave fast together?

chapters.zh: 母狮子在洞中蹲伏，少壮狮子在隐密处埋伏；你能为他们抓取食物，使他们饱足么？
chapters.en: Wilt thou hunt the prey for the lion? or fill the appetite of the young lions,

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: When they couch in dens, abide in the covert to lie in wait?

chapters.zh: 乌鸦之雏因无食物飞来飞去，哀告神；那时，谁为他预备食物呢？
chapters.en: Who provideth for the raven his food? when his young ones cry unto God, they wander for lack of meat.

chapters.zh: 山岩间的野山羊几时生产，你知道么？母鹿下犊之期，你能察定么？
chapters.en: Knowest thou the time when the wild goats of the rock bring forth? canst thou mark when the hinds do calve?

chapters.zh: 他们怀胎的月数，你能数算么？他们几时生产，你能晓得么？
chapters.en: Canst thou number the months they fulfil? or knowest thou the time when they bring forth?

chapters.zh: 他们屈身，将子生下，就除掉疼痛。
chapters.en: They bow themselves, they bring forth their young ones, they cast out their sorrows.

chapters.zh: 这子渐渐肥壮，在荒野长大，去而不回。
chapters.en: Their young ones are in good liking, they grow up with corn; they go forth, and return not unto them.

chapters.zh: 谁放野驴出去自由？谁解开快驴的绳索？
chapters.en: Who hath sent out the wild ass free? or who hath loosed the bands of the wild ass?

chapters.zh: 我使旷野作他的住处，使咸地当他的居所。
chapters.en: Whose house I have made the wilderness, and the barren land his dwellings.

chapters.zh: 他嗤笑城内的喧嚷，不听赶牲口的喝声。
chapters.en: He scorneth the multitude of the city, neither regardeth he the crying of the driver.

chapters.zh: 遍山是他的草场；他寻找各样青绿之物。
chapters.en: The range of the mountains his pasture, and he searcheth after every green thing.

chapters.zh: 野牛岂肯服事你？岂肯住在你的槽旁？
chapters.en: Will the unicorn be willing to serve thee, or abide by thy crib?

chapters.zh: 你岂能用套绳将野牛笼在犁沟之间？他岂肯随你耙山谷之地？
chapters.en: Canst thou bind the unicorn with his band in the furrow? or will he harrow the valleys after thee?

chapters.zh: 岂可因他的力大就倚靠他？岂可把你的工交给他做么？
chapters.en: Wilt thou trust him, because his strength great? or wilt thou leave thy labour to him?

chapters.zh: 岂可信靠他把你的粮食运到家，又收聚你禾场上的谷么？
chapters.en: Wilt thou believe him, that he will bring home thy seed, and gather thy barn?

chapters.zh: 鸵鸟的翅膀欢然搧展，岂是显慈爱的翎毛和羽毛么？
chapters.en: the goodly wings unto the peacocks? or wings and feathers unto the ostrich?

chapters.zh: 因他把蛋留在地上，在尘土中使得温暖；
chapters.en: Which leaveth her eggs in the earth, and warmeth them in dust,

chapters.zh: 却想不到被脚踹碎，或被野兽践踏。
chapters.en: And forgetteth that the foot may crush them, or that the wild beast may break them.

chapters.zh: 他忍心待雏，似乎不是自己的；虽然徒受劳苦，也不为雏惧怕；
chapters.en: She is hardened against her young ones, as though not hers: her labour is in vain without fear;

chapters.zh: 因为神使他没有智慧，也未将悟性赐给他。
chapters.en: Because God hath deprived her of wisdom, neither hath he imparted to her understanding.

chapters.zh: 他几时挺身展开翅膀，就嗤笑马和骑马的人。
chapters.en: What time she lifteth up herself on high, she scorneth the horse and his rider.

chapters.zh: 马的大力是你所赐的么？他颈项上挓挲的鬃是你给他披上的么？
chapters.en: Hast thou given the horse strength? hast thou clothed his neck with thunder?

chapters.zh: 是你叫他跳跃像蝗虫么？他喷气之威使人惊惶。
chapters.en: Canst thou make him afraid as a grasshopper? the glory of his nostrils terrible.

chapters.zh: 他在谷中刨地，自喜其力；他出去迎接佩带兵器的人。
chapters.en: He paweth in the valley, and rejoiceth in strength: he goeth on to meet the armed men.

chapters.zh: 他嗤笑可怕的事并不惊惶，也不因刀剑退回。
chapters.en: He mocketh at fear, and is not affrighted; neither turneth he back from the sword.

chapters.zh: 箭袋和发亮的枪，并短枪在他身上铮铮有声。
chapters.en: The quiver rattleth against him, the glittering spear and the shield.

chapters.zh: 他发猛烈的怒气将地吞下；一听角声就不耐站立。
chapters.en: He swalloweth the ground with fierceness and rage: neither believeth he that the sound of the trumpet.

chapters.zh: 角每发声，他说呵哈；他从远处闻著战气，又听见军长大发雷声和兵丁呐喊。
chapters.en: He saith among the trumpets, Ha, ha; and he smelleth the battle afar off, the thunder of the captains, and the shouting.

chapters.zh: 鹰雀飞翔，展开翅膀一直向南，岂是藉你的智慧么？
chapters.en: Doth the hawk fly by thy wisdom, stretch her wings toward the south?

chapters.zh: 大鹰上腾在高处搭窝，岂是听你的吩咐么？
chapters.en: Doth the eagle mount up at thy command, and make her nest on high?

chapters.zh: 他住在山岩，以山峰和坚固之所为家，
chapters.en: She dwelleth and abideth on the rock, upon the crag of the rock, and the strong place.

chapters.zh: 从那里窥看食物，眼睛远远观望。
chapters.en: From thence she seeketh the prey, her eyes behold afar off.

chapters.zh: 他的雏也咂血；被杀的人在哪里，他也在那里。
chapters.en: Her young ones also suck up blood: and where the slain , there she.

chapters.zh: 耶和华又对约伯说：
chapters.en: Moreover the LORD answered Job, and said,

chapters.zh: 强辩的岂可与全能者争论么？与神辩驳的可以回答这些罢！
chapters.en: Shall he that contendeth with the Almighty instruct ? he that reproveth God, let him answer it.

chapters.zh: 于是，约伯回答耶和华说：
chapters.en: Then Job answered the LORD, and said,

chapters.zh: 我是卑贱的！我用甚么回答你呢？只好用手摀口。
chapters.en: Behold, I am vile; what shall I answer thee? I will lay mine hand upon my mouth.

chapters.zh: 我说了一次，再不回答；说了两次，就不再说。
chapters.en: Once have I spoken; but I will not answer: yea, twice; but I will proceed no further.

chapters.zh: 于是，耶和华从旋风中回答约伯说：
chapters.en: Then answered the LORD unto Job out of the whirlwind, and said,

chapters.zh: 你要如勇士束腰；我问你，你可以指示我。
chapters.en: Gird up thy loins now like a man: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.

chapters.zh: 你岂可废弃我所拟定的？岂可定我有罪，好显自己为义么？
chapters.en: Wilt thou also disannul my judgment? wilt thou condemn me, that thou mayest be righteous?

chapters.zh: 你有神那样的膀臂么？你能像他发雷声么？
chapters.en: Hast thou an arm like God? or canst thou thunder with a voice like him?

chapters.zh: 你要以荣耀庄严为妆饰，以尊荣威严为衣服；
chapters.en: Deck thyself now majesty and excellency; and array thyself with glory and beauty.

chapters.zh: 要发出你满溢的怒气，见一切骄傲的人，使他降卑；
chapters.en: Cast abroad the rage of thy wrath: and behold every one proud, and abase him.

chapters.zh: 见一切骄傲的人，将他制伏，把恶人践踏在本处；
chapters.en: Look on every one proud, bring him low; and tread down the wicked in their place.

chapters.zh: 将他们一同隐藏在尘土中，把他们的脸蒙蔽在隐密处；
chapters.en: Hide them in the dust together; bind their faces in secret.

chapters.zh: 我就认你右手能以救自己。
chapters.en: Then will I also confess unto thee that thine own right hand can save thee.

chapters.zh: 你且观看河马；我造你也造他。他吃草与牛一样；
chapters.en: Behold now behemoth, which I made with thee; he eateth grass as an ox.

chapters.zh: 他的气力在腰间，能力在肚腹的筋上。
chapters.en: Lo now, his strength in his loins, and his force in the navel of his belly.

chapters.zh: 他摇动尾巴如香柏树；他大腿的筋互相联络。
chapters.en: He moveth his tail like a cedar: the sinews of his stones are wrapped together.

chapters.zh: 他的骨头好像铜管；他的肢体彷佛铁棍。
chapters.en: His bones strong pieces of brass; his bones like bars of iron.

chapters.zh: 他在神所造的物中为首；创造他的给他刀剑。
chapters.en: He the chief of the ways of God: he that made him can make his sword to approach .

chapters.zh: 诸山给他出食物，也是百兽游玩之处。
chapters.en: Surely the mountains bring him forth food, where all the beasts of the field play.

chapters.zh: 他伏在莲叶之下，卧在芦苇隐密处和水洼子里。
chapters.en: He lieth under the shady trees, in the covert of the reed, and fens.

chapters.zh: 莲叶的阴凉遮蔽他；溪旁的柳树环绕他。
chapters.en: The shady trees cover him their shadow; the willows of the brook compass him about.

chapters.zh: 河水泛滥，他不发战；就是约但河的水涨到他口边，也是安然。
chapters.en: Behold, he drinketh up a river, hasteth not: he trusteth that he can draw up Jordan into his mouth.

chapters.zh: 在他防备的时候，谁能捉拿他？谁能牢笼他穿他的鼻子呢？
chapters.en: He taketh it with his eyes: nose pierceth through snares.

chapters.zh: 你能用鱼钩钓上鳄鱼么？能用绳子压下他的舌头么？
chapters.en: Canst thou draw out leviathan with an hook? or his tongue with a cord thou lettest down?

chapters.zh: 你能用绳索穿他的鼻子么？能用钩穿他的腮骨么？
chapters.en: Canst thou put an hook into his nose? or bore his jaw through with a thorn?

chapters.zh: 他岂向你连连恳求，说柔和的话么？
chapters.en: Will he make many supplications unto thee? will he speak soft unto thee?

chapters.zh: 岂肯与你立约，使你拿他永远作奴仆么？
chapters.en: Will he make a covenant with thee? wilt thou take him for a servant for ever?

chapters.zh: 你岂可拿他当雀鸟玩耍么？岂可为你的幼女将他拴住么？
chapters.en: Wilt thou play with him as a bird? or wilt thou bind him for thy maidens?

chapters.zh: 搭伙的渔夫岂可拿他当货物么？能把他分给商人么？
chapters.en: Shall the companions make a banquet of him? shall they part him among the merchants?

chapters.zh: 你能用倒钩枪扎满他的皮，能用鱼叉叉满他的头么？
chapters.en: Canst thou fill his skin with barbed irons? or his head with fish spears?

chapters.zh: 你按手在他身上，想与他争战，就不再这样行罢！
chapters.en: Lay thine hand upon him, remember the battle, do no more.

chapters.zh: 人指望捉拿他是徒然的；一见他，岂不丧胆么？
chapters.en: Behold, the hope of him is in vain: shall not be cast down even at the sight of him?

chapters.zh: 没有那么凶猛的人敢惹他。这样，谁能在我面前站立得住呢？
chapters.en: None fierce that dare stir him up: who then is able to stand before me?

chapters.zh: 谁先给我甚么，使我偿还呢？天下万物都是我的。
chapters.en: Who hath prevented me, that I should repay under the whole heaven is mine.

chapters.zh: 论到鳄鱼的肢体和其大力，并美好的骨骼，我不能缄默不言。
chapters.en: I will not conceal his parts, nor his power, nor his comely proportion.

chapters.zh: 谁能剥他的外衣？谁能进他上下牙骨之间呢？
chapters.en: Who can discover the face of his garment? who can come with his double bridle?

chapters.zh: 谁能开他的腮颊？他牙齿四围是可畏的。
chapters.en: Who can open the doors of his face? his teeth terrible round about.

chapters.zh: 他以坚固的鳞甲为可夸，紧紧合闭，封得严密。
chapters.en: scales pride, shut up together a close seal.

chapters.zh: 这鳞甲一一相连，甚至气不得透入其间，
chapters.en: One is so near to another, that no air can come between them.

chapters.zh: 都是互相联络、胶结，不能分离。
chapters.en: They are joined one to another, they stick together, that they cannot be sundered.

chapters.zh: 他打喷嚏就发出光来；他眼睛好像早晨的光线（原文是眼皮）。
chapters.en: By his neesings a light doth shine, and his eyes like the eyelids of the morning.

chapters.zh: 从他口中发出烧著的火把，与飞迸的火星；
chapters.en: Out of his mouth go burning lamps, sparks of fire leap out.

chapters.zh: 从他鼻孔冒出烟来，如烧开的锅和点著的芦苇。
chapters.en: Out of his nostrils goeth smoke, as of a seething pot or caldron.

chapters.zh: 他的气点著煤炭，有火燄从他口中发出。
chapters.en: His breath kindleth coals, and a flame goeth out of his mouth.

chapters.zh: 他颈项中存著劲力；在他面前的都恐吓蹦跳。
chapters.en: In his neck remaineth strength, and sorrow is turned into joy before him.

chapters.zh: 他的肉块互相联络，紧贴其身，不能摇动。
chapters.en: The flakes of his flesh are joined together: they are firm in themselves; they cannot be moved.

chapters.zh: 他的心结实如石头，如下磨石那样结实。
chapters.en: His heart is as firm as a stone; yea, as hard as a piece of the nether .

chapters.zh: 他一起来，勇士都惊恐，心里慌乱，便都昏迷。
chapters.en: When he raiseth up himself, the mighty are afraid: by reason of breakings they purify themselves.

chapters.zh: 人若用刀，用枪，用标枪，用尖枪扎他，都是无用。
chapters.en: The sword of him that layeth at him cannot hold: the spear, the dart, nor the habergeon.

chapters.zh: 他以铁为干草，以铜为烂木。
chapters.en: He esteemeth iron as straw, brass as rotten wood.

chapters.zh: 箭不能恐吓他使他逃避；弹石在他看为碎秸。
chapters.en: The arrow cannot make him flee: slingstones are turned with him into stubble.

chapters.zh: 棍棒算为禾秸；他嗤笑短枪飕的响声。
chapters.en: Darts are counted as stubble: he laugheth at the shaking of a spear.

chapters.zh: 他肚腹下如尖瓦片；他如钉耙经过淤泥。
chapters.en: Sharp stones under him: he spreadeth sharp pointed things upon the mire.

chapters.zh: 他使深渊开滚如锅，使洋海如锅中的膏油。
chapters.en: He maketh the deep to boil like a pot: he maketh the sea like a pot of ointment.

chapters.zh: 他行的路随后发光，令人想深渊如同白发。
chapters.en: He maketh a path to shine after him; would think the deep hoary.

chapters.zh: 在地上没有像他造的那样，无所惧怕。
chapters.en: Upon earth there is not his like, who is made without fear.

chapters.zh: 凡高大的，他无不藐视；他在骄傲的水族上作王。
chapters.en: He beholdeth all high : he a king over all the children of pride.

chapters.zh: 约伯回答耶和华说：
chapters.en: Then Job answered the LORD, and said,

chapters.zh: 我知道，你万事都能做；你的旨意不能拦阻。
chapters.en: I know that thou canst do every , and no thought can be withholden from thee.

chapters.zh: 谁用无知的言语使你的旨意隐藏呢？我所说的是我不明白的；这些事太奇妙，是我不知道的。
chapters.en: Who he that hideth counsel without knowledge? therefore have I uttered that I understood not; things too wonderful for me, which I knew not.

chapters.zh: 求你听我，我要说话；我问你，求你指示我。
chapters.en: Hear, I beseech thee, and I will speak: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.

chapters.zh: 我从前风闻有你，现在亲眼看见你。
chapters.en: I have heard of thee by the hearing of the ear: but now mine eye seeth thee.

chapters.zh: 因此我厌恶自己（或译：我的言语），在尘土和炉灰中懊悔。
chapters.en: Wherefore I abhor , and repent in dust and ashes.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对约伯说话以后，就对提幔人以利法说：我的怒气向你和你两个朋友发作，因为你们议论我不如我的仆人约伯说的是。
chapters.en: And it was , that after the LORD had spoken these words unto Job, the LORD said to Eliphaz the Temanite, My wrath is kindled against thee, and against thy two friends: for ye have not spoken of me right, as my servant Job .

chapters.zh: 现在你们要取七只公牛，七只公羊，到我仆人约伯那里去，为自己献上燔祭，我的仆人约伯就为你们祈祷。我因悦纳他，就不按你们的愚妄办你们。你们议论我，不如我的仆人约伯说的是。
chapters.en: Therefore take unto you now seven bullocks and seven rams, and go to my servant Job, and offer up for yourselves a burnt offering; and my servant Job shall pray for you: for him will I accept: lest I deal with you folly, in that ye have not spoken of me right, like my servant Job.

chapters.zh: 于是提幔人以利法、书亚人比勒达、拿玛人琐法照著耶和华所吩咐的去行；耶和华就悦纳约伯。
chapters.en: So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite Zophar the Naamathite went, and did according as the LORD commanded them: the LORD also accepted Job.

chapters.zh: 约伯为他的朋友祈祷。耶和华就使约伯从苦境（原文是掳掠）转回，并且耶和华赐给他的比他从前所有的加倍。
chapters.en: And the LORD turned the captivity of Job, when he prayed for his friends: also the LORD gave Job twice as much as he had before.

chapters.zh: 约伯的弟兄、姊妹，和以先所认识的人都来见他，在他家里一同吃饭；又论到耶和华所降与他的一切灾祸，都为他悲伤安慰他。每人也送他一块银子和一个金环。
chapters.en: Then came there unto him all his brethren, and all his sisters, and all they that had been of his acquaintance before, and did eat bread with him in his house: and they bemoaned him, and comforted him over all the evil that the LORD had brought upon him: every man also gave him a piece of money, and every one an earring of gold.

chapters.zh: 这样，耶和华后来赐福给约伯比先前更多。他有一万四千羊，六千骆驼，一千对牛，一千母驴。
chapters.en: So the LORD blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning: for he had fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand she asses.

chapters.zh: 他也有七个儿子，三个女儿。
chapters.en: He had also seven sons and three daughters.

chapters.zh: 他给长女起名叫耶米玛，次女叫基洗亚，三女叫基连哈朴。
chapters.en: And he called the name of the first, Jemima; and the name of the second, Kezia; and the name of the third, Kerenhappuch.

chapters.zh: 在那全地的妇女中找不著像约伯的女儿那样美貌。他们的父亲使他们在弟兄中得产业。
chapters.en: And in all the land were no women found fair as the daughters of Job: and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren.

chapters.zh: 此后，约伯又活了一百四十年，得见他的儿孙，直到四代。
chapters.en: After this lived Job an hundred and forty years, and saw his sons, and his sons' sons, four generations.

chapters.zh: 这样，约伯年纪老迈，日子满足而死。
chapters.en: So Job died, old and full of days.
